You are auditing source code for an INDEPENDENT external code review (client: Telus). Tools: Bash, Read.

STEP 0 — SKIP IF DONE. Run: test -s /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/name_findings/shard_40.tsv && echo DONE || echo TODO
If it prints DONE, this shard is already complete — reply exactly "shard 40: cached" and STOP. Do nothing else.

STEP 1 — get your file list: /opt/conda/bin/python -c "import json; print(chr(10).join(json.load(open('/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/.name_shards.json'))[40]))"
It prints lines "relpath:START-END" relative to /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent.

STEP 2 — GENUINELY READ every assigned file/range line by line with the Read tool (offset/limit from START-END; use limit up to 2000 per call to minimize calls). Do NOT grep-and-skim — read the code.

STEP 3 — flag EVERY name (file/module, class, func/method, var, const, enum value) that looks UNPROFESSIONAL in an external review:
  - marketing/quality adjectives: honest, real, true, smart, magic, ultimate, enhanced, improved, robust, aggressive, killer, awesome, blazing, super, mega, best
  - temporal/version markers: v2/v3/v4, r2/r3, _new, _old, legacy, deprecated, next/nextgen, final(meaning 'this version'), fixed, temp, wip, draft, backup, orig, working, experimental, proto
  - jokes/informal/vague/slang: junk, garbage, hack, spy, stub(as a real name), yolo, stuff, misc, foo/bar, doit, thing, blah, landmine, nuke, magic-number-as-word, terse cryptic abbreviations
Judge at a STRICT bar: **when in doubt, mark RENAME.** Only KEEP-BUT-NOTED for unambiguously standard, descriptive, professional vocabulary or a REAL domain term genuinely implemented (finalize, proper_noun, evict_older_than, SmartArt only if it truly makes SmartArt).

STEP 4 — APPEND TAB-separated rows to /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/name_findings/shard_40.tsv (one per flagged name), using printf with real tabs:
  VERDICT<TAB>NAME<TAB>relpath:LINE<TAB>KIND<TAB>WHY<TAB>SUGGESTED_NAME
  VERDICT=RENAME or KEEP-NOTED; KIND=file/class/func/var/const/enum. If a file is fully clean, write: NONE<TAB>-<TAB>relpath<TAB>-<TAB>clean<TAB>-

Reply ONLY: "shard 40: <R> renames, <K> kept across <F> files".

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	"""
2	Multi-section generator — HONEST-REBUILD Gap-4.
3	
4	Three-stage architecture that produces 1500-3000-word reports while
5	keeping per-section provenance tightness:
6	
7	  1. OUTLINE stage  (1 LLM call, ~500 tokens)
8	     DeepSeek reads all evidence and emits a JSON section plan:
9	       [{"title": "Efficacy", "focus": "...", "ev_ids": ["ev_001", ...]},
10	        {"title": "Safety", "focus": "...", "ev_ids": [...]},
11	        {"title": "Comparative", ...}]
12	     Sections constrained to a fixed allowed set so the model can't
13	     invent topics unsupported by evidence.
14	
15	  2. PER-SECTION GENERATION  (N parallel LLM calls, ~800 tokens each)
16	     Each section gets its own prompt with ONLY its evidence subset +
17	     focus statement. Generates 8-15 sentences with [ev_XXX] markers.
18	
19	  3. VERIFY + OPTIONAL REGEN  (deterministic + 0-N retry calls)
20	     Each section is strict_verified. If <50% sentences kept, the
21	     section is regenerated ONCE with a "tighter citations required"
22	     reminder. If regen still fails, the section is dropped (with a
23	     note in the report).
24	
25	  4. ASSEMBLY
26	     verified_sections + shared Methods + contradictions + Limitations
27	     + bibliography, concatenated.
28	
29	Cost estimate: ~$0.01-$0.02 per report (vs $0.0022 for single-call).
30	"""
31	from __future__ import annotations
32	
33	import asyncio
34	import contextvars
35	import difflib
36	import json
37	import logging
38	import os
39	import re
40	import time
41	from dataclasses import dataclass, field, replace
42	from typing import Any, Optional
43	
44	import httpx
45	
46	from src.polaris_graph.generator.live_deepseek_generator import (
47	    _DECIMAL_RE,
48	    _EV_MARKER_RE,
49	    _rewrite_draft_with_spans,
50	    build_prompt,
51	)
52	from src.polaris_graph.generator.provenance_generator import (
53	    resolve_provenance_to_citations,
54	    resolve_provenance_to_citations_with_count,
55	    sanitize_evidence_text,
56	    strict_verify,
57	    wrap_evidence_for_prompt,
58	)
59	# I-arch-011 #1268 PR-c: per-basket verified-compose (the PRIMARY section-prose producer when
60	# PG_VERIFIED_COMPOSE is on). Re-exported here so callers + the replay harness share one source.
61	from src.polaris_graph.generator.verified_compose import (  # noqa: F401
62	    _ENRICHMENT_TITLE,
63	    _compose_section_per_basket,
64	    _section_baskets_for_compose,
65	    # I-deepfix-001 Wave-1a (#1344): the shared PG_SYNTH_PRIMARY gate (single source of truth) — reused
66	    # here so the branch-selection flag read matches _compose_one_basket's exactly.
67	    _synth_primary_enabled,
68	    _verified_compose_enabled,
69	    build_verified_span_draft,
70	    dedup_same_span_sentences,
71	    # I-deepfix-001 WS-3 (#1344): no-provenance-token leak repair (the drb_72
72	    # no_provenance_token=34 leak) — an untokened abstractive sentence is REPAIRED to
73	    # the nearest supporting basket's verified clause BEFORE strict_verify drops it.
74	    no_token_sentence_repair_enabled,
75	    # I-deepfix-001 Wave-3 PART 2 ARM B P1a (#1344): HOLD the degraded-verify disclosure ASIDE out of the
76	    # strict_verify-bound draft, then RENDER it back onto the section body post-verify (so it is never
77	    # rebound by _repair_untokened_draft nor dropped no_provenance_token by strict_verify).
78	    partition_composed_disclosures,
79	    render_degraded_disclosures,
80	    repair_untokened_sentence,
81	    # I-deepfix-001 F1 (#1344): route EVERY consolidated basket to a section so no verified
82	    # basket is stranded with no home (~600 stranded baskets in drb_72). Default-OFF.
83	    route_all_baskets_enabled,
84	    route_orphan_baskets_to_section_plans,
85	    split_into_sentences,
86	)
87	# I-deepfix-001 (#1344) Bug B: retraction grounding gate — a retracted/withdrawn
88	# source must never ground generated prose (it is excluded from evidence_pool BEFORE
89	# selection / M-44 injection / M-52 pull, then disclosed in run telemetry — §-1.3).
90	from src.polaris_graph.generator import retraction_gate
91	# I-deepfix-001 (#1344) W9: content-dedup CONSOLIDATE-KEEP-ALL — group near-identical-
92	# body syndicated sources into keep-all corroboration baskets (annotate, never drop,
93	# never merge). Wired on the groundable pool so W9 fires on the Gate-B path (§-1.3).
94	from src.polaris_graph.synthesis import content_dedup_consolidate
95	# I-deepfix-001 FIX 5 (#1344): cross-section repetition guard — CONSOLIDATE a finding that recurs
96	# VERBATIM across DIFFERENT sections to its richest instance + a citation-preserving back-reference
97	# (frees section space for DISTINCT findings). RENDER-ONLY, faithfulness-neutral, default-OFF (§-1.3).
98	from src.polaris_graph.generator.cross_section_repetition_guard import (
99	    consolidate_cross_section_repetition,
100	    guard_enabled as cross_section_repetition_guard_enabled,
101	)
102	
103	logger = logging.getLogger("polaris_graph.multi_section")
104	
105	
106	# I-arch-005 B2/B3 (#1257): run-scoped tail-drop telemetry sink for the per-section
107	# character-budget trim. A ContextVar (NOT a module-global list) so concurrent runs do not
108	# cross-contaminate and so it auto-resets per run. ``generate_multi_section_report`` binds a
109	# fresh list at entry; ``_budget_trim_ev_ids`` appends one record per binding tail-drop;
110	# tests read it via ``_budget_tail_drop_sink()``. Each record names the cap site, the
111	# budget, the kept/in/dropped row counts and the dropped chars — so the char-budget is
112	# OBSERVABLE (the B2_B3 verdict flagged the tail-drop as silent).
113	_BUDGET_TAIL_DROP_TELEMETRY_CTX: contextvars.ContextVar[
114	    "list[dict[str, Any]] | None"
115	] = contextvars.ContextVar("pg_budget_tail_drop_telemetry", default=None)
116	
117	
118	def _budget_tail_drop_sink() -> "list[dict[str, Any]] | None":
119	    """The current run's tail-drop telemetry list (or None when unbound). The four
120	    per-section cap sites pass this into ``_budget_trim_ev_ids(telemetry_sink=...)``."""
121	    return _BUDGET_TAIL_DROP_TELEMETRY_CTX.get()
122	
123	
124	def _build_reliability_header(credibility_analysis: Any) -> dict[str, Any] | None:
125	    """I-arch-005 B6/B8 (#1257): a report-level reliability header from the per-claim
126	    baskets. Counts claims by corroboration STRENGTH using ONLY the
127	    ``verified_support_origin_count`` (the independently span-verified origins — NEVER the
128	    advisory ``total_clustered_origin_count``): corroborated (>= 2 verified origins),
129	    single-origin (exactly 1), and contested (>= 1 refuter cluster). Returns None when no
130	    basket data is present (master flag OFF) => byte-identical. This is a disclosure SIGNAL,
131	    not a gate — it never keeps/drops a sentence."""
132	    baskets = getattr(credibility_analysis, "baskets", None)
133	    if not baskets:
134	        return None
135	    corroborated = 0
136	    single_origin = 0
137	    contested = 0
138	    total_with_verified = 0
139	    for _b in baskets:
140	        _vcount = int(getattr(_b, "verified_support_origin_count", 0) or 0)
141	        _refuters = getattr(_b, "refuter_cluster_ids", ()) or ()
142	        if _refuters:
143	            contested += 1
144	        if _vcount >= 2:
145	            corroborated += 1
146	            total_with_verified += 1
147	        elif _vcount == 1:
148	            single_origin += 1
149	            total_with_verified += 1
150	    return {
151	        "claims_total": len(baskets),
152	        "claims_with_verified_support": total_with_verified,
153	        "claims_multi_source_corroborated": corroborated,
154	        "claims_single_origin": single_origin,
155	        "claims_contested": contested,
156	        # The reliability of a claim is carried by its basket's INDEPENDENTLY span-verified
157	        # supporting origins (a corroboration signal), never by the raw clustered count.
158	        "corroboration_basis": "verified_support_origin_count",
159	    }
160	
161	
162	def _basket_cluster_id(_b: Any) -> str:
163	    """The claim_cluster_id of a basket that may be an object OR a projected dict. PURE."""
164	    if isinstance(_b, dict):
165	        return str(_b.get("claim_cluster_id") or "")
166	    return str(getattr(_b, "claim_cluster_id", "") or "")
167	
168	
169	def _basket_verified_count(_b: Any) -> int:
170	    """The basket's verified_support_origin_count (object OR projected dict). PURE."""
171	    if isinstance(_b, dict):
172	        return int(_b.get("verified_support_origin_count", 0) or 0)
173	    return int(getattr(_b, "verified_support_origin_count", 0) or 0)
174	
175	
176	def _basket_refuters(_b: Any) -> Any:
177	    if isinstance(_b, dict):
178	        return _b.get("refuter_cluster_ids", ()) or ()
179	    return getattr(_b, "refuter_cluster_ids", ()) or ()
180	
181	
182	def build_report_scoped_reliability_header(
183	    baskets: Any, cited_cluster_ids: "set[str] | frozenset[str] | list[str] | None",
184	) -> "dict[str, Any] | None":
185	    """I-deepfix-001 S3 (#1344): a SECOND, REPORT-SCOPED reliability header counting corroboration
186	    strength over ONLY the baskets whose claim_cluster_id is actually CITED in the rendered report
187	    (``cited_cluster_ids``), not the whole evidence pool. The pool-level header (
188	    ``_build_reliability_header``) advertised "286 clusters / 2 multi-source" above a bibliography
189	    whose cited claims were all single-origin — reading as if the CITED claims were corroborated.
190	    This restricts the same accounting to the cited set so the reader sees the corroboration
191	    strength of the claims they can actually read. Returns None when there is no basket data or no
192	    cited set (=> caller renders only the pool header, byte-identical). PURE; a disclosure SIGNAL,
193	    never a gate. Accepts basket OBJECTS or the projected dicts."""
194	    if not baskets or not cited_cluster_ids:
195	        return None
196	    cited = {str(c) for c in cited_cluster_ids}
197	    corroborated = 0
198	    single_origin = 0
199	    contested = 0
200	    total_with_verified = 0
201	    cited_total = 0
202	    for _b in baskets:
203	        if _basket_cluster_id(_b) not in cited:
204	            continue
205	        cited_total += 1
206	        _vcount = _basket_verified_count(_b)
207	        if _basket_refuters(_b):
208	            contested += 1
209	        if _vcount >= 2:
210	            corroborated += 1
211	            total_with_verified += 1
212	        elif _vcount == 1:
213	            single_origin += 1
214	            total_with_verified += 1
215	    return {
216	        "scope": "report_cited",
217	        "claims_total": cited_total,
218	        "claims_with_verified_support": total_with_verified,
219	        "claims_multi_source_corroborated": corroborated,
220	        "claims_single_origin": single_origin,
221	        "claims_contested": contested,
222	        "corroboration_basis": "verified_support_origin_count",
223	    }
224	
225	
226	# B12-COMPLETION (#1257): the disclosed-gap label string used when the credibility pass
227	# cannot run because the production judge / gov_suffixes were not threaded. NAMED constant
228	# (no inline magic string) so the inline guard and the unit test reference the SAME text.
229	_CREDIBILITY_NO_JUDGE_DISCLOSED_GAP: str = (
230	    "credibility_pass_unavailable: the activated credibility analysis could not run "
231	    "(no production credibility judge / gov_suffixes were threaded into generation); "
232	    "sources ship UNSCORED at neutral credibility weight and this gap is disclosed. The "
233	    "binding faithfulness gates (strict_verify, 4-role D8, span-grounding) are unaffected — "
234	    "only the advisory credibility disclosure is degraded."
235	)
236	
237	# I-arch-011 (#1268): the disclosed-gap string for the F2a PRIORS-ONLY *run* path. Distinct from
238	# _CREDIBILITY_NO_JUDGE_DISCLOSED_GAP (the pass-could-NOT-run degrade): here the pass DID run, just
239	# without the LLM credibility judge (judge=None, e.g. PG_CREDIBILITY_LLM_JUDGE=off). Surfaced on the
240	# operator-visible carrier (manifest credibility_disclosed_gap) so priors-only weights never ship
241	# without the promised disclosure (LAW II; Codex I-arch-011 P1). NAMED constant so the run path and
242	# the unit test reference the SAME text.
243	_CREDIBILITY_PRIORS_ONLY_DISCLOSED_GAP: str = (
244	    "credibility_pass_priors_only: the LLM credibility judge was not wired (judge=None, e.g. "
245	    "PG_CREDIBILITY_LLM_JUDGE=off); the credibility pass RAN but scored every source by deterministic "
246	    "authority priors only and labeled them credibility_unscored — this gap is disclosed. The binding "
247	    "faithfulness gates (strict_verify, 4-role D8, span-grounding) are unaffected."
248	)
249	
250	
251	def _credibility_guard_decision(
252	    *, judge: Any, gov_suffixes: Any, always_release: bool,
253	) -> str:
254	    """I-arch-005 B12-COMPLETION (#1257): the PURE decision for the credibility-pass
255	    pre-run guard, extracted so it is directly unit-testable (the inline guard runs deep in
256	    ``generate_multi_section_report`` after the LLM outline/section calls).
257	
258	    Returns:
259	      * ``"run"`` — gov_suffixes ARE threaded; run the pass. A threaded judge scores via the
260	        LLM; a MISSING judge (under always-release) runs the COMPLETE priors-only pass (see the
261	        I-arch-011 note below).
262	      * ``"degrade"`` — gov_suffixes missing AND always-release ON; degrade to the
263	        credibility-OFF path (credibility_analysis stays None) + a disclosed gap + CONTINUE.
264	      * ``"raise"`` — judge OR gov_suffixes missing AND always-release OFF (legacy); the caller
265	        raises CredibilityPassError fail-closed (byte-identical to pre-fix).
266	
267	    B12-COMPLETION made the raise gate on always_release so "label not hold" is live in
268	    production.
269	
270	    I-arch-011 (#1268) BREADTH KEYSTONE: a MISSING judge and MISSING gov_suffixes are NOT the
271	    same condition and must NOT share a branch. ``gov_suffixes`` missing is a real wiring hole —
272	    the pass cannot classify government sources at all, so degrade (always-release) / raise
273	    (legacy) stands. But ``judge=None`` is DIFFERENT: ``run_credibility_analysis(judge=None)``
274	    runs a COMPLETE priors-only pass — ZERO LLM scoring calls (scoring needs a judge), every
275	    source LABELED ``credibility_unscored`` (a disclosed gap, never fabricated) — and that
276	    priors-only pass is exactly what BUILDS the per-claim baskets the breadth-enrichment surfaces.
277	    The old shared ``judge is None or not gov_suffixes`` → ``degrade`` threw the basket away
278	    (credibility_analysis=None), which is the 794→9 cited-source collapse (PG_CREDIBILITY_LLM_JUDGE
279	    is gated off on the run to avoid the side-judge GIL hang, so judge arrives None). So a missing
280	    judge with gov_suffixes present must RUN priors-only under always-release, not degrade. Legacy
281	    (always-release OFF) keeps the byte-identical fail-closed raise. Faithfulness is unchanged:
282	    priors weights are real deterministic authority weights; strict_verify / 4-role D8 /
283	    span-grounding stay the ONLY binding gates."""
284	    if not gov_suffixes:
285	        return "degrade" if always_release else "raise"
286	    if judge is None:
287	        return "run" if always_release else "raise"
288	    return "run"
289	
290	
291	# I-arch-002 (#1248) / I-arch-005 B21 (#1257): per-section wall-clock guard.
292	# Each section's bounded runner is wrapped in asyncio.wait_for so a WEDGED section (a
293	# 0-socket provider stall — observed on the drb_72 smoke: the section asyncio.gather hung
294	# 19 min with NO open sockets / ~0 CPU, immune to the httpx read timeout because no socket
295	# was open to time out) gets a hard wall-clock bound: it RETRIES once (a fresh call likely
296	# hits a healthy provider), then raises a TimeoutError. That TimeoutError is in
297	# ``_TRANSIENT_SECTION_FAILURES``, so ``_gather_sections_isolated`` catches it and emits a
298	# VISIBLE gap-stub for THAT section (``_section_failure_to_gap_stub``) — a section hang is a
299	# disclosed gap, never a hung run. Faithfulness-neutral: only affects WHETHER a section
300	# generates; strict_verify / NLI / 4-role / provenance are untouched.
301	#
302	# B21 (#1257) flips this to DEFAULT-ON. Pre-fix the default was 0 (OFF) — a hung section
303	# could hang the whole report forever on any caller that did NOT set the env (only the
304	# Gate-B cert slate set it). The default MUST exceed the inner generator LLM per-call timeout
305	# (GENERATOR_TIMEOUT_SECONDS default 600s, openrouter_client.py) + verify/rewrite headroom, or
306	# it would fire on a slow-but-legitimate section and burn the retry.
307	#
308	# B24 (#1257) RIGHT-SIZES this default from 9000s to 1800s (30 min). 9000s was the cert-slate
309	# value (sized for the old 6500s inner timeout) frozen into the MODULE default; that made every
310	# non-slate caller (dev / smoke / ad-hoc) wait up to 2.5h on a true hang before the gap stub
311	# fired. 1800s = the 30-min section backstop: comfortably above a slow-but-legit section yet a
312	# sane dev default. The Gate-B cert slate INDEPENDENTLY floors this UP to >= 9000s for the real
313	# certification run (run_gate_b.py apply_full_capability_benchmark_slate + a fail-loud preflight),
314	# so cert-run completeness is UNCHANGED — only the non-slate default comes down. Env-overridable
315	# (PG_SECTION_WALLCLOCK_SECONDS); set <= 0 to restore the legacy no-wrap path (the byte-identical
316	# escape hatch). Faithfulness-neutral: only affects WHETHER a section generates; strict_verify /
317	# NLI / 4-role / provenance are untouched.
318	PG_SECTION_WALLCLOCK_SECONDS_DEFAULT: str = "1800"

321	def _section_wallclock_seconds() -> int:
322	    try:
323	        return int(os.getenv(
324	            "PG_SECTION_WALLCLOCK_SECONDS", PG_SECTION_WALLCLOCK_SECONDS_DEFAULT
325	        ))
326	    except ValueError:
327	        return int(PG_SECTION_WALLCLOCK_SECONDS_DEFAULT)

330	# COMPLETENESS-CRITIC fix (I-deepfix-001 round-2): the run-wall deadline as an absolute
331	# ``time.monotonic()`` instant. The spine (run_honest_sweep_r3.run_one_query) sets this from
332	# its own ``_RUN_WALL_CLOCK_DEADLINE_CTX`` right before generation so the per-section guard
333	# can cap each ``wait_for`` by the REMAINING run-wall budget and fire the gap-stub on the
334	# FIRST attempt when a second attempt can no longer fit inside the run-wall. Without this the
335	# section guard does up to ``2 x PG_SECTION_WALLCLOCK_SECONDS`` (slate=9000 => 18000s) while
336	# the whole run is wrapped in ``asyncio.wait_for(run-wall=10800)`` — so a wedged section is
337	# GUILLOTINED by the run-wall (status=error_unexpected, NO rendered report) at 10800s, BEFORE
338	# the gap-stub (which only fires after BOTH attempts raise at ~18000s) can ever render. A
339	# ContextVar (NOT a module global) so concurrent runs stay isolated; default None => the
340	# legacy bare ``min(wall, ...)`` is skipped and behaviour is byte-identical (non-benchmark /
341	# dev / smoke callers that never set it).
342	_RUN_WALL_DEADLINE_CTX: "contextvars.ContextVar[float | None]" = contextvars.ContextVar(
343	    "pg_msg_run_wall_deadline", default=None
344	)
345	
346	
347	def set_run_wall_deadline(deadline_monotonic):
348	    """Public setter the spine calls (``multi_section_generator.set_run_wall_deadline``)
349	    with the absolute ``time.monotonic()`` run-wall instant so the per-section wall-clock
350	    guard can cooperate with the run-wall. Returns the reset token (caller resets in a
351	    finally). ``None`` clears it (byte-identical legacy path)."""
352	    return _RUN_WALL_DEADLINE_CTX.set(deadline_monotonic)
353	
354	
355	def reset_run_wall_deadline(token) -> None:
356	    """Reset the run-wall deadline ctx (paired with ``set_run_wall_deadline``)."""
357	    try:
358	        _RUN_WALL_DEADLINE_CTX.reset(token)
359	    except (ValueError, LookupError):
360	        # A reset against a token from a different context is a no-op, never fatal.
361	        pass

363	
364	async def _run_section_with_wallclock(runner, plan):
365	    """Wrap a section bounded-runner in a per-section wall-clock guard (I-arch-002
366	    #1248). OFF (<= 0) => exact ``await runner(plan)`` (byte-identical). ON => one
367	    wait_for, one retry, then fail-loud on a persistent wedge. ``runner`` re-runs the
368	    full section on retry (re-acquires the semaphore + re-does the work); a cancelled
369	    first attempt appends nothing, so there is no double-write.
370	
371	    COMPLETENESS-CRITIC fix (I-deepfix-001 round-2): when the spine has published a
372	    run-wall deadline (``set_run_wall_deadline``), cap EACH ``wait_for`` by
373	    ``min(wall, remaining_run_wall_budget)`` and SKIP attempt 2 when the remaining budget
374	    cannot fit a second full attempt. This guarantees the section raises a TimeoutError —
375	    and the caller's ``_gather_sections_isolated`` converts it into a VISIBLE gap-stub —
376	    BEFORE the run-wall guillotine (which would otherwise emit error_unexpected with NO
377	    rendered report). Faithfulness-neutral: a gap-stub section never fabricates; the
378	    strict_verify / NLI / 4-role / provenance gates are untouched."""
379	    wall = _section_wallclock_seconds()
380	    if wall <= 0:
381	        return await runner(plan)
382	    last_exc: BaseException | None = None
383	    for attempt in (1, 2):
384	        # Cap this attempt by the REMAINING run-wall budget so the gap-stub fires before
385	        # the run-wall guillotine. Margin keeps a sliver for the gap-stub assembly +
386	        # downstream render to actually run after the section raises.
387	        effective = float(wall)
388	        run_deadline = _RUN_WALL_DEADLINE_CTX.get()
389	        if run_deadline is not None:
390	            try:
391	                _gap_margin = float(os.getenv("PG_SECTION_RUNWALL_MARGIN_S", "120"))
392	            except ValueError:
393	                _gap_margin = 120.0
394	            remaining = run_deadline - time.monotonic() - _gap_margin
395	            if remaining <= 0:
396	                # No budget left for even a partial attempt — gap-stub NOW so the run-wall
397	                # never gets to guillotine an unrendered report.
398	                logger.warning(
399	                    "[gen-wallclock] run-wall budget exhausted before section attempt "
400	                    "%d/2 — gap-stubbing immediately so a report still renders",
401	                    attempt,
402	                )
403	                last_exc = last_exc or TimeoutError("run-wall budget exhausted")
404	                break
405	            effective = min(effective, remaining)
406	            # If even a full ``wall`` second attempt cannot fit, do NOT start one — let
407	            # the first failure gap-stub now rather than risk the run-wall guillotine.
408	            if attempt == 2 and remaining < float(wall):
409	                logger.warning(
410	                    "[gen-wallclock] insufficient run-wall budget for a 2nd full "
411	                    "%ds attempt (%.0fs remaining) — failing loud now so the gap-stub "
412	                    "renders before the run-wall guillotine",
413	                    wall, remaining,
414	                )
415	                break
416	        try:
417	            return await asyncio.wait_for(runner(plan), timeout=effective)
418	        except asyncio.TimeoutError as exc:
419	            last_exc = exc
420	            logger.warning(
421	                "[gen-wallclock] section exceeded %.0fs wall-clock (attempt %d/2) — "
422	                "likely a transient provider stall; %s",
423	                effective, attempt, "retrying" if attempt == 1 else "failing loud",
424	            )
425	    raise TimeoutError(
426	        f"section generation exceeded {wall}s wall-clock (run-wall-aware) — failing loud "
427	        f"instead of hanging the report (I-arch-002 #1248 / I-deepfix-001 round-2)"
428	    ) from last_exc

432	#
433	# Read at CALL time (NOT an import-time constant / default-arg) so the gate is
434	# monkeypatch-testable per run and so the OFF path is byte-identical: when unset,
435	# every generator source cap (PG_OUTLINE_MAX_EV menu truncation + the FOUR/FIVE
436	# PG_MAX_EV_PER_SECTION row clamps) keeps its exact legacy literal, and OFF is byte-
437	# identical to today. When ON, those ROW caps dissolve into a serialized CHARACTER
438	# budget against the real 1M-context model — sources flow to composition bounded by
439	# the prompt budget + the faithfulness gate, never by a row count (the DNA: WEIGHT-
440	# AND-CONSOLIDATE, not FILTER-AND-CAP). Faithfulness (strict_verify / NLI / 4-role) is
441	# downstream of full-pool text resolution and is untouched.
442	def _credibility_redesign_enabled() -> bool:
443	    """True iff PG_SWEEP_CREDIBILITY_REDESIGN is on. Mirrors the hoisted
444	    ``_cred_redesign_on`` boolean in the sweep runner and
445	    ``credibility_pass.credibility_redesign_enabled`` so ONE flag coherently governs
446	    the whole migration.
447	
448	    P0-A20 (I-arch-007, 602->22 funnel): UNSET now evaluates ON (default ``"on"``) so the
449	    redesign is the coherent default, matching the run-path mirror
450	    (``run_honest_sweep_r3._cred_redesign_on`` already defaults ``"on"``). Pre-fix the empty-string
451	    default fell through the truthy on-list to False, so this generator mirror stayed OFF while the
452	    orchestrator believed the redesign was on — the source-funnel split. An EXPLICIT
453	    ``PG_SWEEP_CREDIBILITY_REDESIGN=0`` (off/false/no) still returns False -> byte-identical legacy
454	    path."""
455	    return (
456	        os.getenv("PG_SWEEP_CREDIBILITY_REDESIGN", "on").strip().lower()
457	        not in ("", "0", "false", "off", "no")

463	# the budget dissolution rode the redesign master flag, so the canonical Gate-B path (and
464	# any non-cert caller) got the LIVE 150/40 ROW caps — the WEIGHT-AND-CONSOLIDATE DNA
465	# violation §-1.3 names. This is a SEPARATE, default-ON budget predicate (NOT a flip of
466	# ``_credibility_redesign_enabled()``, whose two out-of-lane mirrors — evidence_selector +
467	# run_honest_sweep_r3 — must stay coherent). It governs ONLY the four per-section +

471	# generator, never a row count; every assigned row whose serialized statement+direct_quote
472	# fits flows to composition. The faithfulness engine (strict_verify / NLI / 4-role) is
473	# downstream and untouched. ESCAPE HATCH: PG_GEN_ROW_CAPS in {1,true,yes,on} restores the
474	# legacy ROW caps (byte-identical regression path). The preflight (run_gate_b.py) FAILS

477	def _section_budgets_enabled() -> bool:
478	    """True (DEFAULT) iff the character-budget path governs the per-section + outline cap
479	    sites — i.e. the legacy ROW caps are DISSOLVED into character budgets for every caller.

482	    explicit PG_SWEEP_CREDIBILITY_REDESIGN=1 + PG_GEN_ROW_CAPS=1 still gets budgets, the
483	    cert behaviour). Read at CALL time for monkeypatch-testability."""

485	        return True
486	    return (
487	        os.getenv("PG_GEN_ROW_CAPS", "").strip().lower()
488	        not in ("1", "true", "yes", "on")

510	# ── RACE-FLOOR lever 2 (#1344 R2): WRITER-MENU top-N ev-density cap ───────────────────────────────
511	# THE DRAG (drb_72 R2 render, MEASURED): with PG_ROUTE_ALL_BASKETS + PG_EV_BUDGET_TRACKS_PAYLOAD ON,
512	# route_all/facet-route APPEND ~orphan tails so each body section's ``ev_ids`` balloons to 52-103 rows
513	# (vs the deep step3 run's ~15/section). The abstractive writer is then prompted over that crammed
514	# menu and strict_verify DROPS ~65% of what it writes (post-M-41c kept_fraction=0.35) -> THIN prose
515	# (2141 body words vs step3's 3230) and 102 dropped sentences. This cap FOCUSES the WRITER's PROMPT
516	# menu to the top-N highest-ranked rows (``section.ev_ids`` is already ranked: reserved-facet +
517	# authority-ordered head first, route_all/facet-route orphans appended to the TAIL), so the writer
518	# composes deep, well-grounded prose over the primaries exactly like the step3 draw.
519	#
520	# §-1.3 / FAITHFULNESS (why this is provably SAFE — not a source drop, not a verify weakening):
521	#   * It caps ONLY ``ev_subset`` — the rows the WRITER is PROMPTED with. It does NOT touch
522	#     ``section.ev_ids`` (bibliography + credibility disclosure are built from that, so every routed
523	#     row STAYS cited-eligible + disclosed) and does NOT touch ``evidence_pool``.
524	#   * strict_verify / ``_rewrite_draft_with_spans`` gate every emitted sentence against the FULL
525	#     ``evidence_pool`` (NOT ``ev_subset``) — so a capped-out row can still GROUND a sentence the
526	#     writer happened to write, and no sentence is ever admitted that the full pool cannot verify.
527	#     The frozen faithfulness engine (strict_verify / NLI / [#calc] / fold-in) is UNTOUCHED.
528	#   * The selection is DETERMINISTIC (head-N of the already-ranked list) and DISCLOSED (LOUD
529	#     activation log naming the withheld count per section).
530	# Default-OFF (``PG_WRITER_TOPN_EV_PER_SECTION`` unset / <=0) => ``ev_subset`` is the full assigned
531	# set => byte-identical legacy render.
532	def _writer_topn_ev_per_section() -> int:
533	    """Top-N cap on the per-section WRITER evidence menu (``ev_subset``). ``PG_WRITER_TOPN_EV_PER_SECTION``
534	    read at call time (monkeypatch-testable). <=0 / non-integer => 0 => the cap is OFF (byte-identical:
535	    the writer sees every assigned row)."""
536	    try:
537	        v = int(os.getenv("PG_WRITER_TOPN_EV_PER_SECTION", "0").strip())
538	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
539	        return 0
540	    return v if v > 0 else 0

543	def _apply_writer_menu_cap(
544	    ev_subset: list, *, section_title: str = "", total_assigned: int = 0
545	) -> list:
546	    """Return the top-N head of ``ev_subset`` (the WRITER prompt menu) per
547	    ``_writer_topn_ev_per_section()``. Returns a NEW list (never mutates the input, so the caller's
548	    ``section.ev_ids`` / ``evidence_pool`` are never touched). Cap<=0 or a shorter menu => the input
549	    list unchanged (byte-identical). Discloses the withheld tail LOUD when it bites."""
550	    cap = _writer_topn_ev_per_section()
551	    if cap <= 0 or len(ev_subset) <= cap:
552	        return ev_subset
553	    withheld = len(ev_subset) - cap
554	    logger.info(
555	        "[multi_section] %s RACE-FLOOR writer-menu top-N cap: prompting writer with top %d/%d "
556	        "assigned row(s); %d tail row(s) WITHHELD FROM THE WRITER PROMPT ONLY (kept in "
557	        "evidence_pool + bibliography + credibility disclosure; strict_verify still gates every "
558	        "rendered sentence against the FULL pool — faithfulness engine untouched)",
559	        section_title, cap, total_assigned or len(ev_subset), withheld,
560	    )
561	    return ev_subset[:cap]

564	# I-arch-002 (#1246) P-W4gen: per-section serialized CHARACTER budget that REPLACES the
565	# PG_MAX_EV_PER_SECTION row cap under the redesign flag. Read at CALL time. Default is a
566	# generous slice of the 1M-context generator's window (~120K chars ≈ a large fraction of
567	# the context) so a section keeps every assigned row whose serialized
568	# statement+direct_quote fits — never a row count. The faithfulness engine re-verifies
569	# every emitted sentence regardless of how many rows reach the prompt.
570	PG_SECTION_EV_CHAR_BUDGET_DEFAULT: str = "120000"
571	
572	
573	def _section_ev_char_budget() -> int:
574	    """Per-section character budget (statement+direct_quote serialized) for the
575	    redesign no-row-cap path. Read at call time; non-positive => effectively unbounded
576	    (a huge ceiling) so the budget never silently drops a row."""
577	    try:
578	        val = int(os.getenv("PG_SECTION_EV_CHAR_BUDGET", PG_SECTION_EV_CHAR_BUDGET_DEFAULT))
579	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
580	        val = int(PG_SECTION_EV_CHAR_BUDGET_DEFAULT)
581	    if val <= 0:
582	        return 1 << 62  # disabled => no char bound
583	    return val

585	
586	def _ev_serialized_len(row: dict[str, Any]) -> int:
587	    """Serialized length of one evidence row for the per-section char budget:
588	    len(statement) + len(direct_quote). Used to decide how many rows fit a section's
589	    character budget under the redesign flag (replacing the row-count cap)."""
590	    if not isinstance(row, dict):
591	        return 0
592	    stmt = row.get("statement") or ""
593	    quote = row.get("direct_quote") or ""
594	    return len(str(stmt)) + len(str(quote))
595	
596	
597	def _ev_char_len_by_id(evidence: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> dict[str, int]:
598	    """Build an ev_id -> serialized-char-length map from the FULL evidence pool so the
599	    per-section budget trim (which operates on ev_id lists) can size each candidate."""
600	    out: dict[str, int] = {}
601	    for row in evidence:
602	        eid = row.get("evidence_id", "") if isinstance(row, dict) else ""
603	        if eid:
604	            out[eid] = _ev_serialized_len(row)
605	    return out

608	def _budget_trim_ev_ids(
609	    ev_ids: list[str],
610	    char_len_by_id: dict[str, int],
611	    budget: int,
612	    *,
613	    reserved_floor: int = 0,
614	    telemetry_sink: list[dict[str, Any]] | None = None,
615	    site: str = "",
616	) -> list[str]:
617	    """Keep ev_ids in order until the cumulative serialized char budget is reached;
618	    ALWAYS keep at least ``reserved_floor`` leading rows (the SACRED reserved set is
619	    never truncated by the budget). Replaces the row-count clamp on the redesign path:
620	    bounded by characters, never by a row count.
621	
622	    I-arch-005 B2/B3 (#1257): when a TAIL is dropped past the character budget, record it
623	    (count + dropped serialized chars + the kept/in counts + the budget + site) so the
624	    char-budget is OBSERVABLE, not a silent drop. The verdict doc (B2_B3) flagged that this
625	    tail-drop was silent. ``telemetry_sink`` (an append-only list of dicts) receives one
626	    record per binding trim; ``site`` names the cap site. When no sink is provided, the
627	    drop is still logged at WARNING."""
628	    if not ev_ids:
629	        return ev_ids
630	    kept: list[str] = []
631	    used = 0
632	    for idx, eid in enumerate(ev_ids):
633	        size = char_len_by_id.get(eid, 0)
634	        if idx < reserved_floor:
635	            # Reserved rows are sacred — always kept, and they still consume budget so
636	            # the filler accounting stays honest.
637	            kept.append(eid)
638	            used += size
639	            continue
640	        if used + size > budget and kept:
641	            break
642	        kept.append(eid)
643	        used += size
644	    n_dropped = len(ev_ids) - len(kept)
645	    if n_dropped > 0:
646	        dropped_ids = ev_ids[len(kept):]
647	        dropped_chars = sum(char_len_by_id.get(_e, 0) for _e in dropped_ids)
648	        record = {
649	            "site": site or "unknown",
650	            "reason": "char_budget_exceeded",
651	            "budget": int(budget),
652	            "rows_in": len(ev_ids),
653	            "rows_kept": len(kept),
654	            "rows_dropped": n_dropped,
655	            "chars_used": used,
656	            "chars_dropped": dropped_chars,
657	        }
658	        if telemetry_sink is not None:
659	            telemetry_sink.append(record)
660	        logger.warning(
661	            "[gen-budget] tail-drop at %s: kept %d/%d rows (budget=%d chars, "
662	            "used=%d, dropped=%d rows / %d chars) — char-budget bound, not a row cap",
663	            record["site"], len(kept), len(ev_ids), budget, used, n_dropped, dropped_chars,
664	        )
665	    return kept

668	# D-1 / I-ready-017 (#1182): per-section + analyst-synthesis CONTENT token budget.
669	#
670	# The default generator (deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro per I-cd-009 Carney lock) is
671	# REASONING-FIRST: it emits 6k-42k+ reasoning tokens BEFORE any content. A small
672	# hardcoded ceiling (the prior magic `4000`) starved the content phase, so
673	# finish_reason=length truncated and the FX-01 (#1105) reasoning->content
674	# promotion guard correctly REFUSED to ship the scratchpad — dropping whole
675	# narrative sections. Per LAW VI this is a NAMED, env-overridable module constant
676	# (no magic number). Default is deliberately generous so a reasoning-first writer
677	# has room to FINISH planning AND write the cited paragraph.
678	#
679	# IMPORTANT (scope honesty): openrouter_client clamps every reasoning-first
680	# request to clamp(request, PG_REASONING_FIRST_MIN_MAX_TOKENS, PG_REASONING_FIRST_HARD_CAP).
681	# I-arch-003 (#1253, operator 2026-06-14 "generation tokens go max max"): the hard cap is now
682	# 384000 (the generator chain is pinned to fp8 FULL-CAP providers >= 384,000; DeepInfra fp4/16384
683	# is EXCLUDED), and the floor is 32768. So this constant is now ACTIVE room (not the old
684	# forward-compat headroom): a value between 32768 and 384000 is honored verbatim. Raised
685	# 24000 -> 64000 so long clinical sections get ~46k content room on top of V4 Pro's ~18k
686	# reasoning burn — directly serving the completeness lever (POLARIS loses to ChatGPT on
687	# completeness, not faithfulness). Bounded WELL below 384000 on purpose: max_tokens is a
688	# usage-billed CEILING (no cost when unused), but a per-section ceiling of 384k risks a runaway
689	# multi-minute section that hurts the wall-clock the operator also prioritizes; 64000 is the
690	# zero-starvation / no-runaway balance. The truncation GUARD (FX-01 promotion path in
691	# openrouter_client) is untouched — we widen the content budget, never disable the
692	# refusal-to-ship-scratchpad guard.
693	PG_SECTION_MAX_TOKENS: int = int(os.getenv("PG_SECTION_MAX_TOKENS", "64000"))
694	
695	# V30 Phase-2 contract-slot extraction floor (M-66 run-5): contract slots echo
696	# long regulatory prose spans as JSON; they need at least this much budget even
697	# if a caller passes a smaller per-section value. Used as max(section_max_tokens,
698	# floor). Named per LAW VI; openrouter_client still clamps reasoning-first to
699	# PG_REASONING_FIRST_HARD_CAP.
700	PG_CONTRACT_SLOT_MIN_MAX_TOKENS: int = int(
701	    os.getenv("PG_CONTRACT_SLOT_MIN_MAX_TOKENS", "6000")
702	)
703	
704	# I-arch-004 F02 (#1255) — contract-slot reasoning + stall budgets.
705	#
706	# WHY: the V30 contract-slot calls (JSON slot-fill / regulatory synthesis / the
707	# <=3-sentence narrative paragraph) ran through `generate()` with the full
708	# PG_SECTION_MAX_TOKENS=64000 ceiling. deepseek-v4-pro is reasoning-first and (per
709	# openrouter_client.py:1721) reasons until the OVERALL max_tokens ceiling, so these
710	# TERSE calls burned ~8k+ reasoning tokens for a 3-sentence output — wasteful and, on
711	# a slow provider band, a runaway that ends in a degenerate blank (the drb_72 death).
712	#
713	# These calls are extraction/short-narrative — they do NOT need deep reasoning. Cap the
714	# REASONING sub-budget tight (PG_CONTRACT_SLOT_REASONING_MAX_TOKENS) so the model stops
715	# planning early, while keeping the CONTENT budget ample (PG_CONTRACT_SLOT_MIN_MAX_TOKENS
716	# floor preserved) — this SERVES operator-lock §9.1.8 ("never starve content"), it does
717	# NOT relax it: content stays generous, only the wasteful reasoning runaway is bounded.
718	# The global reasoning-first floor/cap and the section-writer/judge budgets are UNTOUCHED.
719	#
720	# A per-call STALL timeout WELL UNDER the section wall (GENERATOR_TIMEOUT_SECONDS=6500s)
721	# bounds a hung terse call so it cannot consume the whole section budget on a stall.
722	#

800	# outline (evidence_selector relevance-floor + Gate-B tier-balanced selection), so a top-N
801	# slice keeps exactly the rows the sections prioritize and drops only the low-relevance tail
802	# that no section would cite. `allowed_ev_ids` validation, full-text resolution
803	# (evidence_pool[ev_id]), the deterministic fallback, the M-44/M-52 primary-anchor
804	# injection, and the per-section PG_MAX_EV_PER_SECTION selection ALL stay on the FULL pool.
805	# Faithfulness gates (strict_verify / NLI / 4-role) are downstream of full-pool text
806	# resolution and are untouched.
807	#
808	# DEFAULT 150 is COVERAGE-FAVORING: sized ABOVE the realized OFF-mode section demand
809	# (~120 ev_ids = 5-6 sections x 12-20 each) so the planner still sees every row a section
810	# would pick — that is what keeps per-section selection effectively unchanged in OFF mode
811	# (where the section ev_ids ARE the outline LLM's picks from this menu). On the LARGE-pool
812	# branch the per-row digest is also TERSED (ev_id + tier + title only; the 160-char
813	# statement is dropped) because the outline only PLANS section structure, so the statement
814	# text is not needed there; tersing roughly halves per-row chars, widening reasoning
815	# headroom at the same N. Env-tunable; read at CALL time (not import) so the cap and digest
816	# are tunable per-run and unit-testable.
817	#
818	# HONEST SCOPE / SIZING CAVEAT: the two bounds do NOT yet provably coincide at 150.
819	#   * coverage LOWER bound: N >= ~120 (section demand) — 150 clears this with headroom.
820	#   * truncation UPPER bound: argued from a SINGLE known-good datapoint (53 VERBOSE rows
821	#     worked pre-a030b024 ~= 13K menu chars; 544 verbose failed). 150 TERSE rows ~= 16-17K
822	#     menu chars — i.e. ~20-25% LARGER than the only known-good input, NOT demonstrably
823	#     within it. So 150 is chosen for coverage, and the truncation fit is a HYPOTHESIS that
824	#     a live V4 Pro 1-query canary must confirm; it is NOT proven by this offline diff.
825	#   * If the canary truncates at 150, the documented levers (in priority order) are: lower
826	#     PG_OUTLINE_MAX_EV toward ~120 (where the two bounds nearly coincide), then the Novita
827	#     no-row-cut route (raise PG_REASONING_FIRST_HARD_CAP to 32000 + pin

941	# Structure-ONLY: it constrains the ORDER and the presence of four structural roles; the thematic
942	# body TITLES and COUNT still emerge from the evidence. Topic-agnostic by construction.
943	_FACET_SKELETON_ADDENDUM = """
944	
945	GENERAL RESEARCH-REPORT SKELETON (structure the plan as a COMPLETE report, not a flat bag of topics):
946	Order the sections so the report reads as a coherent scholarly review with these FOUR structural roles present in EVERY report, regardless of topic:
947	  1. OPEN with exactly ONE framing/overview section that orients the reader to the question, the scope, and the body of evidence (a good title names the subject, e.g. "Introduction and Scope of the Evidence" or "Overview of <subject>"). Assign it broad, high-level or foundational evidence.
948	  2. THEN the THEMATIC BODY sections — one per distinct facet the evidence genuinely supports (as instructed above). These titles EMERGE from the evidence; there is no fixed menu and no fixed count.
949	  3. Include exactly ONE cross-cutting SYNTHESIS section that reads ACROSS the thematic bodies and surfaces where studies AGREE, where they CONTRADICT each other, and how strong/consistent the overall evidence is (e.g. "Cross-Study Synthesis and Contradictions"). Assign it the evidence whose findings converge or conflict.
950	  4. CLOSE with exactly ONE section covering CONCLUSIONS and open RESEARCH GAPS (e.g. "Conclusions and Research Gaps"): what the evidence collectively supports, and what remains unresolved or under-studied.
951	The four ROLES (open / thematic bodies / synthesis+contradictions / conclusions+gaps) are REQUIRED; the thematic-body titles are free to emerge from the evidence. Each section — including the four structural ones — still needs at least 2 genuinely supporting evidence IDs; assign the relevant rows, never fabricate a section the evidence cannot ground."""

1018	# BudgetExceededError), and programming/config/schema defects (AttributeError, NoEndpointError,
1019	# ReasoningFirstTruncationError, FileNotFoundError, ...) — PROPAGATES, so a real bug is never masked as
1020	# a content gap and the cost-cap / faithfulness gates fail FAST. (asyncio.TimeoutError == builtin

1024	# re-raises after MAX_RETRIES (openrouter_client.py:2046-2059, I-transport-001 #1191 — itself a drb_72

1040	def _section_failure_to_gap_stub(plan, exc: BaseException) -> "SectionResult":
1041	    """I-arch-004 A1 (#1248): build a VISIBLE gap-stub SectionResult for a TRANSIENT section failure
1042	    (the wall-clock-x2 TimeoutError, a socket/connection stall). Called ONLY from the isolation
1043	    wrappers, which catch ONLY ``_TRANSIENT_SECTION_FAILURES`` — hard gates + programming defects
1044	    never reach here, they propagate fail-fast (Codex diff-gate iter-1 P1-2/P1-3).
1045	
1046	    ``dropped_due_to_failure=False`` so assembly RENDERS the gap (the ``if not sr.dropped_due_to_failure``
1047	    filter keeps it — Codex diff-gate iter-1 P1-1); ``is_gap_stub=True`` + zero verified sentences so no
1048	    consumer treats it as verified prose. Mirrors the no-evidence gap-stub render pattern."""
1049	    logger.warning(
1050	        "[gen-crash-isolation] section %r transient failure (%s: %s) -> VISIBLE gap-stub; "
1051	        "siblings + gates continue", getattr(plan, "title", "?"), type(exc).__name__, exc,
1052	    )
1053	    return SectionResult(
1054	        title=getattr(plan, "title", ""),
1055	        focus=getattr(plan, "focus", ""),
1056	        ev_ids_assigned=getattr(plan, "ev_ids", []),
1057	        raw_draft="", rewritten_draft="",
1058	        verified_text=_SECTION_FAILED_GAP_STUB_SENTENCE, biblio_slice=[],
1059	        sentences_verified=0, sentences_dropped=0,
1060	        regen_attempted=False, dropped_due_to_failure=False,
1061	        error=f"section_generation_failed: {type(exc).__name__}: {exc}"[:500],
1062	        archetype=getattr(plan, "archetype", ""),
1063	        is_gap_stub=True,
1064	    )

1067	async def _gather_sections_isolated(plans, runner_for):
1068	    """I-arch-004 A1 (#1248): run one bounded section task per plan with per-section TRANSIENT crash
1069	    isolation. Returns a ``list[SectionResult]`` index-aligned with ``plans``.
1070	
1071	    A TRANSIENT failure (``_TRANSIENT_SECTION_FAILURES``: wall-clock-x2 TimeoutError, socket/connection
1072	    stall) is caught INSIDE the task and returned as a visible gap-stub, so one stalled section no
1073	    longer cancels its siblings (the drb_72 death). EVERY other exception — the hard gates
1074	    (``CredibilityPassError`` / ``BudgetExceededError``) AND programming/config/schema defects —
1075	    PROPAGATES out of the plain ``asyncio.gather``, which CANCELS the sibling tasks (fail-fast) and
1076	    aborts the run. So the cost-cap / faithfulness gates fail FAST (no extra sibling spend) and a real
1077	    defect is never silently swallowed (Codex diff-gate iter-1 P1-2/P1-3)."""
1078	    async def _isolated(plan):
1079	        try:
1080	            return await runner_for(plan)
1081	        except _TRANSIENT_SECTION_FAILURES as exc:
1082	            return _section_failure_to_gap_stub(plan, exc)
1083	
1084	    # Codex diff-gate iter-2 P1-3: a plain asyncio.gather propagates the first exception but does NOT
1085	    # cancel the still-running siblings — a hard gate (BudgetExceededError / CredibilityPassError) or a
1086	    # programming defect would otherwise let sibling section calls keep SPENDING in the live loop. Drive
1087	    # explicit tasks and CANCEL the pending ones on any non-transient exception before re-raising, then
1088	    # drain the cancellations cleanly. Transient failures never reach here (caught inside _isolated).
1089	    tasks = [asyncio.ensure_future(_isolated(p)) for p in plans]
1090	    try:
1091	        return await asyncio.gather(*tasks)
1092	    except BaseException:
1093	        for _t in tasks:
1094	            if not _t.done():
1095	                _t.cancel()
1096	        await asyncio.gather(*tasks, return_exceptions=True)
1097	        raise

1101	# These TAGS are the on-path control-flow key — a non-clinical question gets a
1102	# question-specific TITLE plus one of these tags, and on-mode audit routing
1103	# (M-44 / M-47) consults the tag, never a clinical title literal. The set is
1104	# domain-agnostic: a housing, physics, or trade question maps cleanly onto it.
1105	SECTION_ARCHETYPES: list[str] = [
1106	    "Background",
1107	    "Mechanism",
1108	    "Quantitative-Comparison",
1109	    "Cost-Economics",
1110	    "Risk",
1111	    "Jurisdiction",
1112	    "Stakeholders",
1113	    "Scenarios",
1114	    "Decision",
1115	    "Uncertainty",
1116	    "Methodology",
1117	    # I-meta-005 Phase 6 (#990, Codex ruling B-impl-1 / shape 1): VERIFIED
1118	    # cross-cutting synthesis. A normal planned outline section — generated +
1119	    # strict_verified like any other (emits [ev_XXX] tokens; ungrounded
1120	    # synthesis sentences are DROPPED, never laundered into verified). The
1121	    # planner allocates broad/cross-cutting evidence to it and it synthesizes
1122	    # ONLY from its allocated evidence. This REPLACES the unverified
1123	    # analyst_synthesis block on-mode (which is demoted).
1124	    "Integrative",
1125	    "Limitations",
1126	]

1129	# I-meta-005 Phase 1 (#985, brief §2.3): config-driven advisory prompt-text
1130	# selector for the on-path. A frame's field-invariant `claim_type` selects an
1131	# advisory prose family from the `config/section_prompts/_registry.yaml`
1132	# mapping. This is the ONLY clinical-prose seam, and it is NOT a control value:
1133	# the registry is config (LAW VI), the appended text is advisory-only, and the
1134	# archetype outline / parser / fallback / routing are byte-identical regardless
1135	# of which (if any) family is appended. There is no `if claim_type ==
1136	# "clinical"` literal in this code.
1137	_SECTION_PROMPTS_REGISTRY_PATH = os.getenv(
1138	    "PG_SECTION_PROMPTS_REGISTRY",
1139	    os.path.join("config", "section_prompts", "_registry.yaml"),
1140	)
1141	
1142	
1143	def select_advisory_prompt_text(
1144	    claim_type: str, answer_type: str = "general",
1145	) -> str:
1146	    """Return the advisory prompt-text for a frame, or "" when no family is
1147	    registered. Pure config lookup — no clinical literal as a control value;
1148	    fail-soft to "" when the registry is absent (advisory text is enrichment,
1149	    not a gate).
1150	
1151	    I-meta-005 Phase 6 (#990, Codex ruling A1): consult `by_answer_type` FIRST
1152	    (the explicit domain-category the planner now emits), then `by_claim_type`
1153	    (Phase 1, currently unmapped), then `default`. So clinical writing guidance
1154	    is appended ONLY for a clinical answer_type — a non-clinical empirical
1155	    question gets none."""
1156	    import yaml  # local import: advisory enrichment, keep module surface lean
1157	
1158	    registry_path = _SECTION_PROMPTS_REGISTRY_PATH
1159	    if not os.path.isfile(registry_path):
1160	        return ""
1161	    try:
1162	        with open(registry_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as fh:
1163	            registry = yaml.safe_load(fh) or {}
1164	    except (OSError, yaml.YAMLError) as exc:
1165	        logger.warning(
1166	            "[multi_section] advisory prompt registry load failed: %s", exc,
1167	        )
1168	        return ""
1169	    by_answer_type = registry.get("by_answer_type") or {}
1170	    by_claim_type = registry.get("by_claim_type") or {}
1171	    akey = (answer_type or "").strip().lower()
1172	    ckey = (claim_type or "").strip().lower()
1173	    # answer_type (explicit domain) wins over claim_type (generic shape).
1174	    filename = (
1175	        by_answer_type.get(akey)
1176	        or by_claim_type.get(ckey)
1177	        or registry.get("default")
1178	    )
1179	    if not filename:
1180	        return ""
1181	    family_path = os.path.join(os.path.dirname(registry_path), str(filename))
1182	    if not os.path.isfile(family_path):
1183	        return ""
1184	    try:
1185	        with open(family_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as fh:
1186	            family = yaml.safe_load(fh) or {}
1187	    except (OSError, yaml.YAMLError):
1188	        return ""
1189	    return str(family.get("advisory_prompt_text", "") or "")
