6768	    # prose was empty (is_gap_stub), the DISTINCT degraded disclosure REPLACES the generic gap stub (the
6769	    # honest, specific disclosure the operator wants) while is_gap_stub STAYS True so the section is still
6770	    # treated as non-verified prose. Empty when ARM B is OFF => byte-identical.
6771	    if _vc_degraded_disclosures:
6772	        verified_text = render_degraded_disclosures(
6773	            "" if is_gap_stub else verified_text, _vc_degraded_disclosures,
6774	        )
6775	
6776	    # B2 (I-deepfix-001 #1344): per-section BOUNDARY-CONDITIONS / COUNTER-EVIDENCE line. When the
6777	    # section rendered real verified prose, synthesize ONE marker-less disclosure that quotes a
6778	    # LOWER-WEIGHT basket which qualifies or bounds a headline claim (WEIGHT-IN, not filter-out) —
6779	    # surfacing opposition already present in the weighted corpus even when no refuter CLUSTER exists.
6780	    # Appended AFTER strict_verify, quoting an already-verified span; never a new claim fed to the
6781	    # faithfulness engine. Default-ON kill-switch; fail-open. A gap-stub section (no verified prose)
6782	    # gets no boundary line — there is no headline to bound.
6783	    if not is_gap_stub and verified_text and _vc_baskets:
6784	        try:
6785	            from src.polaris_graph.generator.boundary_conditions import (  # noqa: PLC0415
6786	                boundary_conditions_enabled as _b2_enabled,
6787	                synthesize_boundary_line as _b2_line,
6788	            )
6789	            if _b2_enabled():
6790	                _b2_text = _b2_line(_vc_baskets, _vc_baskets)
6791	                if _b2_text:
6792	                    verified_text = verified_text + _b2_text
6793	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — additive disclosure; never break the section render
6794	            pass

6796	    # I-deepfix-001 (Codex grpC iter2 P1) — gap-stub verified-accounting must ship ZERO
6797	    # claims to the binding D8 four-role gate. A gap-stub section renders ONLY the
6798	    # marker-less gap-disclosure stub (a non-claim). When the U24 numeric-cite screen
6799	    # (or the resolver) EMPTIED the body, the pre-fix code still returned
6800	    # sentences_verified=resolved_emitted (non-zero) and
6801	    # kept_sentences_pre_resolve=list(report.kept_sentences) (the WITHHELD SVs). Those
6802	    # withheld sentences then re-entered the binding gate via build_native_gate_b_inputs
6803	    # (native_gate_b_inputs.py) as verified D8 claims — a faithfulness hole (the uncited
6804	    # numeric claims we deliberately withheld from the render were still judged as
6805	    # verified prose). Zero the verified-count and CLEAR the pre-resolve kept list for
6806	    # the gap-stub case, and account the withheld sentences as DROPPED (effective_verified

6808	    # withheld sentence is counted as a drop, keeping the counter honest). A normal
6809	    # (non-gap-stub) section is byte-identical: effective_verified == resolved_emitted and
6810	    # kept_pre_resolve == list(report.kept_sentences). The frozen faithfulness engine
6811	    # (strict_verify / NLI / provenance / D8 four-role logic) is UNTOUCHED — this only
6812	    # EXCLUDES the withheld claims from what is FED to D8. It STRENGTHENS faithfulness.

6814	    kept_pre_resolve = [] if is_gap_stub else list(report.kept_sentences)

6817	    # drops in sentences_dropped so the section-level total matches
6818	    # what verification_details.json serializes (strict + dedup + m41c).
6819	    m41c_drop_count = len(report_dropped_m41c) if report_dropped_m41c else 0

6821	        title=section.title,
6822	        focus=section.focus,
6823	        ev_ids_assigned=section.ev_ids,

6826	        verified_text=verified_text,
6827	        biblio_slice=biblio_slice,

6892	_TRIAL_SUMMARY_TABLE_HEADER_RE = re.compile(
6893	    r"^\s*\|\s*Trial\s*\|\s*N\s*\|\s*Baseline\s*\|\s*Comparator\s*\|\s*Endpoint"
6894	    r"\s*\|\s*Result\s*\|\s*Ref\s*\|\s*$",
6895	    re.IGNORECASE | re.MULTILINE,
6896	)
6897	_MARKDOWN_TABLE_SEPARATOR_RE = re.compile(
6898	    r"^\s*\|(?:\s*:?-+:?\s*\|)+\s*$", re.MULTILINE,
6899	)
6900	_CITATION_MARKER_RE = re.compile(r"\[(\d+)\]")

6903	def _table_cell_verify_enabled() -> bool:
6904	    """I-ready-015 (#1084): cell-decimal faithfulness gate. Default OFF -> byte-identical;
6905	    turned ON + preflighted in the full-capability benchmark slate after audit."""
6906	    return os.environ.get("PG_SWEEP_TABLE_CELL_VERIFY", "").strip().lower() not in {
6907	        "", "0", "false", "off", "no",
6908	    }

6911	def _extract_trial_summary_table(
6912	    raw: str,
6913	    valid_citation_nums: set[int],
6914	    verified_prose: str = "",
6915	) -> str:
6916	    """Extract and validate a `| Trial | ... |`-shaped markdown table
6917	    from an LLM response.
6918	
6919	    Returns the cleaned table text (header + separator + rows) or an
6920	    empty string if the response has no valid table or the only data
6921	    row contains invalid citations.
6922	
6923	    Validation:
6924	      - Table must have the canonical header row.
6925	      - Must have the markdown separator row immediately after.
6926	      - Must have at least 1 data row.
6927	      - Every `[N]` citation marker in ANY data row must reference a
6928	        number present in `valid_citation_nums`. Rows with out-of-
6929	        range citations are dropped. If that leaves zero rows, the
6930	        empty string is returned.
6931	      - The sentinel `NO_TRIALS_NAMED` collapses to empty string.
6932	    """
6933	    if not raw:
6934	        return ""
6935	    text = raw.strip()
6936	    if text == "NO_TRIALS_NAMED":
6937	        return ""
6938	    # Strip code fences if present.
6939	    text = re.sub(r"^```(?:markdown|md)?\s*", "", text)
6940	    text = re.sub(r"\s*```\s*$", "", text)
6941	
6942	    header_match = _TRIAL_SUMMARY_TABLE_HEADER_RE.search(text)
6943	    if not header_match:
6944	        return ""
6945	    # Start from the header line. Collect the header, the separator
6946	    # (which should be the next non-empty line), and subsequent rows
6947	    # until a non-pipe line ends the table.
6948	    #
6949	    # NOTE on header_match.start(): the header regex opens with `\s*`
6950	    # which, under MULTILINE, may consume the `\n` immediately before
6951	    # the header line. That makes `text[header_match.start():]` begin
6952	    # with a leading `\n`, so `splitlines()` yields `[""]` as the
6953	    # first element. We skip leading empty/whitespace lines until we
6954	    # find the actual header row.
6955	    lines_after = text[header_match.start():].splitlines()
6956	    # Skip leading empty lines.
6957	    while lines_after and not lines_after[0].strip():
6958	        lines_after = lines_after[1:]
6959	    if len(lines_after) < 2:
6960	        return ""
6961	    header_line = lines_after[0].strip()
6962	    separator_line = lines_after[1].strip()
6963	    if not _MARKDOWN_TABLE_SEPARATOR_RE.match(separator_line):
6964	        return ""
6965	
6966	    # I-ready-015 (#1084): cell-decimal faithfulness gate (flag-gated, default OFF). The body
6967	    # prose goes through §9.1 strict_verify (every decimal must appear in its cited span), but the
6968	    # LLM-emitted table cells did not — so a mis-transcribed N / HR / endpoint value could survive
6969	    # with only its [N] marker validated. When enabled, every numeric token in a row's DATA cells
6970	    # must appear in the strict_verified `verified_prose` (the table's SOLE fact source); else the
6971	    # number was fabricated/mis-transcribed and the row is dropped. Reuses strict_verify._decimals
6972	    # so the table + prose share one numeric definition. Option B (prose-subset) per Codex brief;
6973	    # Option A (per-[N] span) + Timeline/Per-Trial extractors are documented follow-ups.
6974	    _cell_verify = _table_cell_verify_enabled() and bool(verified_prose.strip())
6975	    _prose_decimals: set[str] = set()
6976	    if _cell_verify:
6977	        from src.polaris_graph.clinical_generator.strict_verify import _decimals as _sv_decimals
6978	        # Codex diff-gate P2: strip [N] markers from the SOURCE prose too (symmetric with the
6979	        # per-row strip below) — otherwise a citation number like [5] becomes a prose "decimal"
6980	        # and a fabricated cell value "5" would falsely pass.
6981	        _prose_decimals = _sv_decimals(_CITATION_MARKER_RE.sub("", verified_prose))
6982	
6983	    kept_rows: list[str] = []
6984	    for line in lines_after[2:]:
6985	        stripped = line.strip()
6986	        if not stripped:
6987	            break
6988	        if not stripped.startswith("|"):
6989	            break
6990	        # Validate citation markers in this row.
6991	        nums = [int(m.group(1)) for m in _CITATION_MARKER_RE.finditer(stripped)]
6992	        if not nums:
6993	            # No [N] in this row → drop. Per rule #1 every row must cite.
6994	            continue
6995	        if any(n not in valid_citation_nums for n in nums):
6996	            # One or more out-of-range citation numbers → drop.
6997	            continue
6998	        if _cell_verify:
6999	            # Strip [N] citation markers FIRST so citation numbers are not treated as data
7000	            # (Codex brief P2), then require every cell decimal to be present in the prose.
7001	            _row_data = _CITATION_MARKER_RE.sub("", stripped)
7002	            if not _sv_decimals(_row_data).issubset(_prose_decimals):
7003	                continue
7004	        # M-41b (2026-04-21, post-V24 Codex pass-12 regression): drop
7005	        # rows where >2 cells contain only "—" / "-" / "–" / empty.
7006	        # Pass-12 audit on V24 observed "table is only 3 rows, 2
7007	        # mostly empty" — the LLM filled the header row but padded
7008	        # later rows with dashes. A row whose half the cells are
7009	        # dashes is worse than no row; it looks like quantified data
7010	        # but conveys nothing. We count cells, not characters; the
7011	        # markdown row syntax "| a | b | c |" splits to 3 content
7012	        # cells after trimming leading/trailing empties.
7013	        cells = [c.strip() for c in stripped.split("|")]
7014	        # Strip leading/trailing empty cells from the split (the
7015	        # outer pipes produce empty first/last elements).
7016	        while cells and cells[0] == "":
7017	            cells = cells[1:]
7018	        while cells and cells[-1] == "":
7019	            cells = cells[:-1]
7020	        # Count "dash-only" cells — any cell whose content after
7021	        # trimming is one of common dash placeholders.
7022	        _DASH_MARKERS = {"—", "-", "–", "N/A", "n/a", "NA", "–", ""}
7023	        dash_count = sum(1 for c in cells if c in _DASH_MARKERS)
7024	        # Trial Summary table has 7 columns (Trial / N / Baseline /
7025	        # Comparator / Endpoint / Result / Ref). Allow up to 2 dash
7026	        # cells out of 7 — 3+ dashes means the row carries too
7027	        # little information to justify the trial-name attribution.
7028	        if dash_count > 2:
7029	            continue
7030	        kept_rows.append(stripped)
7031	
7032	    if not kept_rows:
7033	        return ""
7034	
7035	    return "\n".join([header_line, separator_line, *kept_rows])

7097	# V30 Phase-2 M-66 run-3 acceptance — Trial Summary row
7098	# quality gate. Codex pass-3 CONDITIONAL-no-blockers revision:
7099	# reject rows containing the observed run-2 bad patterns
7100	# (fragment comparators and result-field placeholders), but
7101	# scope narrowly to avoid over-rejecting legitimate rows.
7102	_M66_FRAGMENT_COMPARATOR_RE = re.compile(
7103	    r"\s+in\s+adults\s+with\s+type\s*$",  # truncated NEJM/Lancet
7104	                                           # population boilerplate
7105	    re.IGNORECASE,
7106	)

7108	
7109	def _m66_row_passes_quality_gate(cells: dict[str, str]) -> bool:
7110	    """V30 Phase-2 M-66 run-3 Trial Summary quality gate.
7111	
7112	    Rejects rows whose cells show observed run-2 failure modes:
7113	
7114	    1. comparator ends in "in adults with type" (truncated
7115	       NEJM/Lancet boilerplate like "insulin glargine in adults
7116	       with type"). Legitimate comparators like "semaglutide 1 mg"
7117	       or "insulin glargine" pass.
7118	    2. The effect cell is empty AND the fallback would render as
7119	       bare "at week N" placeholder with no numeric information.
7120	       Legitimate rows have either a real effect string (e.g.
7121	       "-0.45%") OR both a timepoint + some numeric population /
7122	       baseline info already shown in other cells.
7123	
7124	    Returns True to keep the row, False to reject.
7125	
7126	    Scoped narrowly per Codex pass-3 guidance so legitimate rows
7127	    with partial information survive.
7128	    """
7129	    comparator = (cells.get("comparator") or "").strip()
7130	    if _M66_FRAGMENT_COMPARATOR_RE.search(comparator):
7131	        return False
7132	
7133	    effect = (cells.get("effect") or "").strip()
7134	    timepoint = (cells.get("timepoint") or "").strip()
7135	    # If effect is missing AND timepoint is the only other non-
7136	    # empty cell in {baseline, effect, timepoint}, the rendered
7137	    # result becomes `at week {timepoint}` with no digits, which
7138	    # is the observed run-2 junk pattern. Legitimate rows with
7139	    # an effect OR with real baseline info are unaffected.
7140	    if not effect:
7141	        baseline = (cells.get("baseline") or "").strip()
7142	        n = (cells.get("n") or "").strip()
7143	        # Require at least one other numeric cell for a timepoint-
7144	        # only row to survive.
7145	        has_other_numeric = any(
7146	            bool(re.search(r"\d", cell)) for cell in (baseline, n)
7147	        )
7148	        if timepoint and not has_other_numeric:
7149	            return False
7150	
7151	    return True
7152	
7153	
7154	def _m42b_extract_from_quote(quote: str) -> dict[str, str]:
7155	    """Extract 7 frame-element cells from a direct_quote string.
7156	    Returns dict with keys {n, baseline, comparator, dose, endpoint,
7157	    timepoint, effect}. Missing fields are empty strings."""
7158	    if not quote:
7159	        return {k: "" for k in
7160	                ("n", "baseline", "comparator", "dose", "endpoint",
7161	                 "timepoint", "effect")}
7162	    cells: dict[str, str] = {}
7163	
7164	    m = _M42B_PAT_N.search(quote)
7165	    if m:
7166	        cells["n"] = m.group(1) or m.group(2) or ""
7167	    else:
7168	        cells["n"] = ""
7169	
7170	    m = _M42B_PAT_BASELINE.search(quote)
7171	    cells["baseline"] = (m.group(1).strip() if m else "")
7172	
7173	    m = _M42B_PAT_COMPARATOR.search(quote)
7174	    cells["comparator"] = (m.group(1).strip() if m else "")
7175	
7176	    m = _M42B_PAT_DOSE.search(quote)
7177	    cells["dose"] = (m.group(1).strip() if m else "")
7178	
7179	    m = _M42B_PAT_ENDPOINT.search(quote)
7180	    cells["endpoint"] = (m.group(1).strip() if m else "")
7181	
7182	    m = _M42B_PAT_TIMEPOINT.search(quote)

7190	def _m42b_year_from_row(row: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
7191	    """Extract publication year from an evidence row. Tries URL/DOI
7192	    year pattern, then direct_quote, then refetched quote (M-42b
7193	    pass-2 medium). Returns 'yyyy' or empty string."""
7194	    url = (row.get("source_url") or row.get("url") or "")
7195	    # Common DOI/URL year patterns: /2021/, (2021), -2021-
7196	    m = re.search(r"[/(\-_](20\d{2})[/)\-_.]", url)
7197	    if m:
7198	        return m.group(1)
7199	    quote = row.get("direct_quote") or ""
7200	    m = re.search(r"\b(20[0-2]\d)\b", quote[:500])
7201	    if m:
7202	        return m.group(1)
7203	    # Pass-2 medium: check refetched quote when original was thin.
7204	    refetched = row.get("_m42b_refetched_quote") or ""
7205	    m = re.search(r"\b(20[0-2]\d)\b", refetched[:500])
7206	    if m:
7207	        return m.group(1)
7208	    return ""

7211	def _m42b_find_ref_num(row: dict[str, Any], bibliography: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> int | None:
7212	    """Return the [N] citation number from the bibliography that
7213	    corresponds to this evidence row. Match by evidence_id or URL."""
7214	    ev_id = row.get("evidence_id") or ""
7215	    url = row.get("source_url") or row.get("url") or ""
7216	    for entry in bibliography:
7217	        if entry.get("evidence_id") == ev_id and ev_id:
7218	            return entry.get("num")
7219	        if entry.get("url") == url and url:
7220	            return entry.get("num")
7221	    return None

7224	def build_trial_summary_and_timeline_from_evidence(
7225	    selected_rows: list[dict[str, Any]],
7226	    primary_trial_anchors: list[str],

7232	    """M-42b deterministic builder. Consumes selected evidence rows
7233	    (from the generator's evidence_pool) + the sweep's
7234	    `primary_trial_anchors` list + global bibliography. Returns
7235	    `(trial_table_md, timeline_md)` — both markdown strings.
7236

7239	        verbatim quote populated by live_retriever during fetch.
7240	      - Secondary: `row.get("statement")` for disambiguation only.
7241	      - Forbidden: prose from any generated report section.
7242	
7243	    Thin-content fallback:
7244	      - If `direct_quote` < 100 chars AND `refetch_fn` is provided,
7245	        calls `refetch_fn(url)` to fetch a fresh 2000-char extract.
7246	      - If still thin, the row is marked extraction-ineligible
7247	        (skipped).

7249	    Row acceptance:
7250	      - For the TABLE: >=4 of 7 frame cells populated.
7251	      - For the TIMELINE: publication year + trial name + at least
7252	        one non-empty cell (endpoint OR effect).

7254	    Returns empty strings when no rows pass the threshold (caller
7255	    falls back to LLM path).

7259	
7260	    valid_ref_nums = {
7261	        int(e.get("num")) for e in bibliography
7262	        if isinstance(e.get("num"), int)
7263	    }
7264	
7265	    # Per-anchor: find the best primary row + extract cells

7267	    # format: (year_int, trial_name, cells, ref_marker)

7272	        # AND is a primary (M-42e would have tagged it at selection

7275	        # live rows use `statement` not `title`.

7293	        # violated the pass-3 source-content contract (statement is
7294	        # for disambiguation only, never as a standalone extraction

7296	        # M-45 (2026-04-22): when refetch_diagnostics_sink is provided,
7297	        # use the diagnostic-capable refetch variant so the orchestrator
7298	        # can emit refetch_diagnostics.json per Codex pass-2 acceptance.

7300	        if len(quote) < 100 and refetch_fn is not None:
7301	            url = best_row.get("source_url") or best_row.get("url") or ""

7310	                    "attempted": False,
7311	                    "method": "none",
7312	                    "raw_char_count": len(quote),
7313	                    "body_type": "",
7314	                    "eligible": False,
7315	                    "failure_mode": "missing_url",
7316	                    "exception_type": "",
7317	                })
7318	            if url:
7319	                try:
7320	                    # M-45: if sink provided, route through the
7321	                    # diagnostic variant for per-URL telemetry.
7322	                    if refetch_diagnostics_sink is not None:
7323	                        from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.live_retriever import (
7324	                            refetch_for_extraction_with_diagnostics,
7325	                        )
7326	                        refetched, diag = refetch_for_extraction_with_diagnostics(
7327	                            url, 2000,
7328	                        )
7329	                        diag["anchor"] = anchor
7330	                        diag["evidence_id"] = best_row.get("evidence_id", "")

7344	                            "evidence_id": best_row.get("evidence_id", ""),
7345	                            "attempted": True,
7346	                            "eligible": False,
7347	                            "failure_mode": "builder_exception",
7348	                            "exception_type": type(exc).__name__,
7349	                            "raw_char_count": 0,
7350	                            "body_type": "",
7351	                            "method": "none",

7364	        # row validator (Codex pass-3 CONDITIONAL-no-blockers):

7367	        # truncated comparator, bare "at week N" result without

7371	        if not _m66_row_passes_quality_gate(cells):

7374	                "anchor=%r cells=%r (fragment or placeholder-only)",

7377	            continue
7378	
7379	        # Citation marker
7380	        ref_num = _m42b_find_ref_num(best_row, bibliography)
7381	        if ref_num is None or ref_num not in valid_ref_nums:

7383	        ref_marker = f"[{ref_num}]"

7385	        year_str = _m42b_year_from_row(best_row)
7386	        year_int = int(year_str) if year_str else 0
7387	
7388	        table_rows.append((year_int, anchor, cells, ref_marker))
7389	
7390	    if len(table_rows) < 2:
7391	        # Not enough rows for a meaningful table — signal LLM fallback
7392	        logger.info(
7393	            "[multi_section] M-42b deterministic builder yielded %d rows "
7394	            "(below threshold of 2); LLM fallback will be used",
7395	            len(table_rows),
7396	        )
7397	        return "", ""
7398	
7399	    # ─── Render Trial Summary table ────────────────────────────
7400	    table_lines = [
7401	        "| Trial | N | Baseline | Comparator | Endpoint | Result | Ref |",
7402	        "|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|",
7403	    ]
7404	    for _year, trial, cells, ref in table_rows:
7405	        row_cells = [
7406	            trial,
7407	            cells["n"] or "—",
7408	            cells["baseline"] or "—",
7409	            cells["comparator"] or "—",
7410	            cells["endpoint"] or "—",
7411	            cells["effect"] or (f"at week {cells['timepoint']}"
7412	                                if cells["timepoint"] else "—"),
7413	            ref,
7414	        ]
7415	        table_lines.append("| " + " | ".join(row_cells) + " |")
7416	    trial_table_md = "\n".join(table_lines)
7417	
7418	    # ─── Render Trial Program Timeline ─────────────────────────
7419	    # Sort by year ascending; rows with year=0 go to end.
7420	    timeline_entries = sorted(
7421	        table_rows,
7422	        key=lambda r: (r[0] if r[0] else 9999, r[1]),
7423	    )
7424	    timeline_lines = ["| Year | Trial | Key result | Ref |",
7425	                      "|---|---|---|---|"]
7426	    for year, trial, cells, ref in timeline_entries:
7427	        year_str = str(year) if year else "—"
7428	        # Key result: prefer effect size; fall back to endpoint
7429	        key_result = cells["effect"] or cells["endpoint"] or "primary result reported"

7433	    timeline_md = "\n".join(timeline_lines)
7434	
7435	    logger.info(

7555	_M50_MIN_PRIMARIES_FOR_SUBSECTIONS = 2
7556	_M50_SUBSECTION_SYSTEM_PROMPT = """You are writing one PER-TRIAL SUBSECTION for a clinical research report.
7557	
7558	The user will provide:
7559	- Trial name (e.g., a phase-3 trial identifier)

7564	1. N (sample size)

7566	3. Comparator (control arm)
7567	4. Primary endpoint
7568	5. Timepoint
7569	6. Effect estimate WITH uncertainty (CI, SD, or p-value)
7570	7. Safety caveat (key adverse event signal or open-label / sponsorship note)
7571	
7572	Output format:
7573	- Plain prose, one paragraph.
7574	- Cite the primary source with [N] at the end of EACH factual claim.
7575	- Do NOT include a heading — the orchestrator adds "### TRIAL_NAME" around your output.
7576	- Do NOT include ellipses (...) or placeholders — use only verifiable numbers from the quote.
7577	- Do NOT claim findings beyond the quote — if any of the 7 elements is missing from the quote, skip it and mention what's missing in the final sentence.
7578	
7579	Example skeleton (placeholders; do NOT use drug names or study names from this example):
7580	"[TRIAL] enrolled N=[N] [POPULATION] [N]. Participants were randomized to [INTERVENTION] versus [COMPARATOR] [N]. The primary endpoint was [ENDPOINT] at [TIMEPOINT] [N]. [INTERVENTION] reduced [ENDPOINT] by [EFFECT] ([UNCERTAINTY]) versus [COMPARATOR] [N]. Adverse events: [SAFETY_SIGNAL] [N]."

7582	CRITICAL:
7583	- Every sentence must end with [N] citation.
7584	- No extrapolation, no marketing language.
7585	- If the quote does not contain an element, omit the element — do not invent.
7586	"""

7652	def _m50_select_candidate_trials(
7653	    evidence_pool: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
7654	    primary_ev_ids_by_anchor: dict[str, list[str]],
7655	    bibliography: list[dict[str, Any]],
7656	    direct_anchors: set[str],
7657	) -> list[tuple[str, dict[str, Any], int, str]]:
7658	    """M-50 (2026-04-22): select candidate trials for per-trial
7659	    subsections.
7660	
7661	    Returns list of (anchor, primary_row, biblio_num, quote) tuples
7662	    for every anchor that:
7663	      - is in the T2D-direct set (passed by caller via direct_anchors)
7664	      - has ≥1 M-42e-detected primary ev_id in the pool
7665	      - the primary has a direct_quote OR refetched quote ≥100 chars
7666	        (strict contract preserved)
7667	      - the primary ev_id has a matching bibliography entry
7668	
7669	    The `quote` element is the richer of direct_quote / refetched
7670	    (M-47 pass-2 + M-50 pass-2 per Codex audit): length-based select
7671	    so a thin direct_quote does NOT short-circuit the richer refetch.
7672	
7673	    If fewer than _M50_MIN_PRIMARIES_FOR_SUBSECTIONS qualify, returns
7674	    empty list (strict gating — no subsections emitted).
7675	    """
7676	    candidates: list[tuple[str, dict[str, Any], int, str]] = []
7677	    biblio_by_evid: dict[str, int] = {}
7678	    for entry in bibliography:
7679	        evid = entry.get("evidence_id")
7680	        num = entry.get("num")
7681	        if isinstance(evid, str) and isinstance(num, int):
7682	            biblio_by_evid[evid] = num
7683	
7684	    for anchor, ev_ids in primary_ev_ids_by_anchor.items():
7685	        if anchor not in direct_anchors:
7686	            continue  # skip SURMOUNT-1/3/4 (indirect) and any other
7687	        for ev_id in ev_ids:
7688	            row = evidence_pool.get(ev_id)
7689	            if not row:
7690	                continue
7691	            # Pick the RICHER of direct_quote / refetched.
7692	            # M-50 pass-2 (Codex audit blocker): plain `a or b`
7693	            # short-circuits on any non-empty string, so a thin
7694	            # direct_quote would hide a fat refetched quote from
7695	            # downstream `_call_m50_per_trial_subsection`. Carry the
7696	            # selected quote through the candidate tuple so the
7697	            # LLM generator uses the exact same string we validated.
7698	            dq = row.get("direct_quote") or ""
7699	            rq = row.get("_m42b_refetched_quote") or ""
7700	            # Length-based selection: prefer the longer eligible one.
7701	            if len(rq) > len(dq) and len(rq) >= 100:
7702	                quote = rq
7703	            elif len(dq) >= 100:
7704	                quote = dq
7705	            elif len(rq) >= 100:
7706	                quote = rq
7707	            else:
7708	                continue  # neither ≥100 → strict-contract skip
7709	            biblio_num = biblio_by_evid.get(ev_id)
7710	            if not isinstance(biblio_num, int):
7711	                continue
7712	            candidates.append((anchor, row, biblio_num, quote))
7713	            break  # one primary per anchor
7714	
7715	    if len(candidates) < _M50_MIN_PRIMARIES_FOR_SUBSECTIONS:
7716	        return []
7717	    return candidates

7720	async def _call_limitations(
7721	    *,
7722	    tier_fractions: dict[str, float] | None,
7723	    contradictions: list[dict[str, Any]] | None,
7724	    date_range: dict[str, Any] | None,
7725	    model: str,
7726	    temperature: float,
7727	    max_tokens: int,
7728	    uncovered_topics: list[str] | None = None,
7729	    tier_disclosure_override: str | None = None,
7730	) -> tuple[str, int, int]:
7731	    """Generate the Limitations paragraph from pipeline telemetry.
7732	
7733	    No evidence is passed — this paragraph discusses the pipeline, not
7734	    the sources. The telemetry block is the ONLY data the model sees.
7735	    Returns (text, input_tokens, output_tokens).
7736	
7737	    On failure (empty content, malformed, budget exhausted) returns a
7738	    deterministic fallback Limitations paragraph so the report never
7739	    ships without this section.
7740	    """
7741	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.live_deepseek_generator import (
7742	        _format_telemetry_block,
7743	    )
7744	    from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import (
7745	        OpenRouterClient,
7746	        set_reasoning_call_context,
7747	    )
7748	
7749	    # #1242 (Codex iter-1 REQUEST_CHANGES): when a canonical tier-disclosure string is
7750	    # threaded in, the telemetry block emits it VERBATIM (single source of truth) so the
7751	    # model can only quote the SAME tier mix the Methods disclosure quotes — never a
7752	    # re-derived, divergent percentage. None => legacy per-tier derivation (byte-identical).
7753	    telemetry = _format_telemetry_block(
7754	        tier_fractions, contradictions, date_range, uncovered_topics,
7755	        tier_disclosure_override=tier_disclosure_override,
7756	    )
7757	
7758	    prompt = (
7759	        f"Pipeline telemetry (use these numbers verbatim):\n\n"
7760	        f"{telemetry}\n\n"
7761	        f"Write the Limitations: paragraph now, following the rules."
7762	    )
7763	
7764	    client = OpenRouterClient(model=model)
7765	    try:
7766	        # I-gen-004 (#496): tag the Limitations call for the trace sink.
7767	        set_reasoning_call_context(
7768	            section="Limitations", call_type="limitations",
7769	        )
7770	        response = await client.generate(
7771	            prompt=prompt,
7772	            system=LIMITATIONS_SYSTEM_PROMPT,
7773	            # I-wire-009 (#1323): limitations_max_tokens defaults to 400 -> floored to 4096; raise
7774	            # CONTENT and BOUND the GLM-5.2 reasoning pool so the Limitations paragraph has room
7775	            # AFTER reasoning and is never starved to empty by an effort=high prelude.
7776	            max_tokens=max(
7777	                max_tokens, int(os.getenv("PG_LIMITATIONS_MIN_MAX_TOKENS", "6000"))
7778	            ),
7779	            temperature=temperature,
7780	            reasoning_max_tokens=int(
7781	                os.getenv("PG_LIMITATIONS_REASONING_MAX_TOKENS", "2048")
7782	            ),
7783	        )
7784	        text = (response.content or "").strip()
7785	        in_tok = response.input_tokens
7786	        out_tok = response.output_tokens
7787	    except Exception as exc:
7788	        logger.warning("[multi_section] limitations call failed: %s", exc)
7789	        text, in_tok, out_tok = "", 0, 0
7790	    finally:
7791	        if hasattr(client, "close"):
7792	            try:
7793	                await client.close()
7794	            except Exception:
7795	                pass
7796	
7797	    # Fallback: if the model didn't start with "Limitations:", prepend it.
7798	    # If the response is empty or broken, emit a deterministic fallback
7799	    # from the telemetry directly so the report always has Limitations.
7800	    if not text or len(text) < 30:
7801	        fallback_parts = ["Limitations:"]
7802	        if tier_fractions:
7803	            t1 = tier_fractions.get("T1", 0) * 100
7804	            fallback_parts.append(
7805	                f"Only {t1:.0f}% of the corpus is T1 peer-reviewed primary "
7806	                f"research."
7807	            )
7808	        if contradictions:
7809	            # I-deepfix-001: mirror the telemetry-block partition so the deterministic fallback also
7810	            # never claims "sources disagree" for a bucket the engine screened as not-comparable.
7811	            def _nc(c: dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
7812	                return bool(c.get("not_comparable")) or (
7813	                    "[not_comparable]" in str(c.get("predicate", "") or "")
7814	                )
7815	            _cmp = [c for c in contradictions if not _nc(c)]
7816	            _ncmp = [c for c in contradictions if _nc(c)]
7817	            for c in _cmp[:2]:
7818	                subj = c.get("subject", "")
7819	                pred = c.get("predicate", "")
7820	                fallback_parts.append(
7821	                    f"Sources disagree on {subj} / {pred}; the final report "
7822	                    f"discloses the range."
7823	                )
7824	            if _ncmp:
7825	                fallback_parts.append(
7826	                    f"{len(_ncmp)} numeric pairing(s) were screened as not-comparable "
7827	                    f"(different quantity kinds); no cross-source contradiction is asserted."
7828	                )
7829	        if date_range:
7830	            s = date_range.get("start")
7831	            if s:
7832	                fallback_parts.append(
7833	                    f"Evidence horizon begins {s}; earlier literature was "
7834	                    f"excluded."
7835	                )
7836	        text = " ".join(fallback_parts)
7837	        logger.info("[multi_section] Limitations: used deterministic fallback")
7838	    elif not text.lower().startswith("limitations:"):
7839	        text = "Limitations: " + text
7840	
7841	    return text, in_tok, out_tok

7849	def _merge_bibliographies(
7850	    section_slices: list[list[dict[str, Any]]],
7851	) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
7852	    """Merge per-section biblios into a single ordered bibliography,
7853	    remapping section-local citation numbers to global numbers.
7854	
7855	    I-deepfix-002 (#1363): off-topic cite-suppression is scoped to the standalone
7856	    weighted-enrichment selection only (``weighted_enrichment.diagnose_unbound_supports_selection``
7857	    withholds confirmed-off-topic SUPPORTS members from ``ev_ids``). The global
7858	    bibliography numberer is NOT a suppression surface: stripping a global ``[N]``
7859	    here would orphan a citation in already strict_verify-PASSED section prose. So
7860	    this builds the bibliography from whatever is actually cited, unchanged."""
7861	    # Each section's biblio has its own 1-based numbering. We need to
7862	    # renumber globally, but the section's verified_text already has
7863	    # [1][2][3] markers in section-local space.
7864	    # Simpler approach: return the raw per-section biblios flattened,
7865	    # deduped by evidence_id, and let the caller remap the inline
7866	    # markers in a separate pass.
7867	    seen: dict[str, dict[str, Any]] = {}
7868	    for sl in section_slices:
7869	        for entry in sl:
7870	            ev_id = entry.get("evidence_id", "")
7871	            if ev_id and ev_id not in seen:
7872	                seen[ev_id] = dict(entry)
7873	    # Renumber globally
7874	    final: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
7875	    for i, entry in enumerate(seen.values(), 1):
7876	        new_entry = dict(entry)
7877	        new_entry["num"] = i
7878	        final.append(new_entry)
7879	    return final
7880	
7881	
7882	def _remap_section_markers_to_global(
7883	    section_results: list[SectionResult],
7884	    global_biblio: list[dict[str, Any]],
7885	) -> list[str]:
7886	    """Rewrite each section's [N] markers from section-local to global.
7887	
7888	    Returns a list of remapped section prose strings.
7889	
7890	    I-deepfix-002 (#1363): this NEVER drops a marker. Every [N] in already
7891	    strict_verify-PASSED section prose maps to its global number; off-topic
7892	    cite-suppression is handled upstream at the enrichment selection only, so a
7893	    verified section citation is never orphaned here."""
7894	    ev_to_global = {b["evidence_id"]: b["num"] for b in global_biblio}
7895	    remapped: list[str] = []
7896	    for sect in section_results:
7897	        if not sect.verified_text:
7898	            continue
7899	        # Build a mapping section-local-num -> global-num
7900	        local_to_global: dict[int, int] = {}
7901	        for entry in sect.biblio_slice:
7902	            local_num = entry.get("num")
7903	            ev_id = entry.get("evidence_id", "")
7904	            global_num = ev_to_global.get(ev_id)
7905	            if local_num is not None and global_num is not None:
7906	                local_to_global[local_num] = global_num
7907	        text = sect.verified_text
7908	
7909	        # Replace [N] markers using the mapping. Do the replace with a
7910	        # callable to avoid subsequent substitutions clobbering each
7911	        # other (e.g., [1] -> [5] -> [15]).
7912	        def _replace(match: re.Match) -> str:
7913	            n = int(match.group(1))
7914	            g = local_to_global.get(n)
7915	            return f"[{g}]" if g else match.group(0)
7916	
7917	        text = re.sub(r"\[(\d+)\]", _replace, text)
7918	        remapped.append(text)
7919	    return remapped
7920

7922	# I-deepfix-001 U17 (#1335): NEAR-DUPLICATE section-body collapse.
7923	#
7924	# THE DUPLICATE: the "Corroborated Weighted Findings" section (weighted_enrichment.
7925	# build_verified_span_draft, joined-prose) and the numbered "Evidence base" section
7926	# (weighted_enrichment.build_evidence_base_section) are BUILT FROM THE SAME uncapped
7927	# unbound-SUPPORTS ``_wfe.ev_ids`` surface, with the same same-work consolidation, the same
7928	# ``spans_per_source()`` budget and the same verbatim ``_emit_unit`` — so both render the SAME
7929	# verified spans (measured 83-94% identical). Rendering BOTH is pure repetition.
7930	#
7931	# THE COLLAPSE: when the Evidence base body is near-identical to a section already assembled
7932	# (the earlier-ordered Corroborated Weighted Findings), skip the Evidence base append — keep ONE.
7933	# This is a DISPLAY de-duplication, NOT a cap/filter/thinner: the surviving section carries the
7934	# SAME verified spans + the SAME [N] citations (same sources, so breadth is preserved), and each
7935	# entry already passed the FROZEN faithfulness engine (strict_verify / NLI / 4-role D8 / provenance
7936	# / span-grounding) independently before this check ever runs. The frozen engine is UNTOUCHED.
7937	# §-1.3-safe: no source is dropped from evidence_pool or the bibliography; only a redundant SECOND
7938	# rendering of already-cited spans is suppressed. Kill-switch ``PG_SECTION_DEDUP_ENABLED`` (default
7939	# ON); OFF => both sections render => byte-identical legacy output. Threshold
7940	# ``PG_SECTION_DEDUP_SIMILARITY`` (default 0.80) is the difflib ratio over normalized bodies.
7941	_ENV_SECTION_DEDUP_ENABLED = "PG_SECTION_DEDUP_ENABLED"
7942	_ENV_SECTION_DEDUP_SIMILARITY = "PG_SECTION_DEDUP_SIMILARITY"
7943	_SECTION_DEDUP_SIMILARITY_DEFAULT = 0.80
7944	# Markdown scaffolding that carries NO evidence content — stripped before comparing bodies so a
7945	# numbered "1. " list and its joined-prose twin normalize to the SAME token stream.
7946	_SECTION_DEDUP_HEADER_RE = re.compile(r"(?m)^\s{0,3}#{1,6}\s.*$")
7947	_SECTION_DEDUP_ENUM_RE = re.compile(r"(?m)^\s*\d+[.)]\s+")
7948	_SECTION_DEDUP_CITATION_RE = re.compile(r"\[[^\[\]]*\]")
7949	_SECTION_DEDUP_WS_RE = re.compile(r"\s+")
7950	
7951	
7952	def section_dedup_enabled() -> bool:
7953	    """Kill-switch ``PG_SECTION_DEDUP_ENABLED`` (default ON). OFF => near-duplicate sections are
7954	    NOT collapsed => byte-identical legacy output (both sections render)."""
7955	    return os.environ.get(_ENV_SECTION_DEDUP_ENABLED, "1").strip().lower() not in (
7956	        "0", "false", "no", "off",
7957	    )
7958	
7959	
7960	def _section_dedup_similarity_threshold() -> float:
7961	    """Configured difflib similarity threshold (default 0.80). A malformed value falls back to the
7962	    default (fail-safe: never collapses on a bad env value)."""
7963	    raw = os.environ.get(_ENV_SECTION_DEDUP_SIMILARITY, "")
7964	    if not raw.strip():
7965	        return _SECTION_DEDUP_SIMILARITY_DEFAULT
7966	    try:
7967	        val = float(raw.strip())
7968	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
7969	        return _SECTION_DEDUP_SIMILARITY_DEFAULT
7970	    if not (0.0 < val <= 1.0):
7971	        return _SECTION_DEDUP_SIMILARITY_DEFAULT
7972	    return val
7973	
7974	
7975	def _normalize_section_body_for_dedup(text: str) -> str:
7976	    """Reduce a rendered section body to its evidence-content token stream so a numbered list and
7977	    its joined-prose twin compare equal: drop markdown headers, leading "N. " enumeration, and
7978	    ``[N]``/``[ev_id]`` citation markers, then lowercase + collapse whitespace."""
7979	    if not text:
7980	        return ""
7981	    out = _SECTION_DEDUP_HEADER_RE.sub(" ", text)
7982	    out = _SECTION_DEDUP_ENUM_RE.sub("", out)
7983	    out = _SECTION_DEDUP_CITATION_RE.sub(" ", out)
7984	    return _SECTION_DEDUP_WS_RE.sub(" ", out).strip().lower()
7985	
7986	
7987	def _section_body_is_near_duplicate(
7988	    candidate_body: str,
7989	    section_results: "list[SectionResult]",
7990	) -> "Optional[str]":
7991	    """Return the TITLE of the first already-assembled, non-dropped section whose normalized body is
7992	    >= the configured similarity threshold to ``candidate_body`` (else ``None``).
7993	
7994	    Content-only comparison (normalized per ``_normalize_section_body_for_dedup``) so the numbered
7995	    "Evidence base" body matches its joined-prose "Corroborated Weighted Findings" twin despite the
7996	    different formatting. Returns ``None`` when the flag is OFF, the candidate normalizes empty, or
7997	    nothing crosses the threshold."""
7998	    if not section_dedup_enabled():
7999	        return None
8000	    cand_norm = _normalize_section_body_for_dedup(candidate_body)
8001	    if not cand_norm:
8002	        return None
8003	    threshold = _section_dedup_similarity_threshold()
8004	    matcher = difflib.SequenceMatcher()
8005	    matcher.set_seq2(cand_norm)
8006	    for sr in section_results:
8007	        if getattr(sr, "dropped_due_to_failure", False):
8008	            continue
8009	        existing_norm = _normalize_section_body_for_dedup(getattr(sr, "verified_text", "") or "")
8010	        if not existing_norm:
8011	            continue
8012	        matcher.set_seq1(existing_norm)
8013	        # Cheap length prefilter (difflib's own guard) before the O(n*m) ratio.
8014	        if matcher.real_quick_ratio() < threshold or matcher.quick_ratio() < threshold:
8015	            continue
8016	        if matcher.ratio() >= threshold:
8017	            return getattr(sr, "title", "") or "<untitled>"
8018	    return None
8019

8021	# I-deepfix-001 WS-3 (#1344): the [ev_id] marker build_evidence_base_section emits — any bracketed
8022	# non-empty token. Membership in evidence_pool decides whether it is a real evidence marker to
8023	# resolve into a [N] (a stray literal bracket is left untouched). Single-pass so a freshly-produced
8024	# [N] is never re-matched.
8025	_EVIDENCE_BASE_MARKER_RE = re.compile(r"\[([^\[\]]+)\]")
8026	
8027	
8028	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
8029	# I-deepfix-006 PT11 — compose-time numeric-citation guarantee (suppress-only)
8030	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
8031	# Mirrors the external_evaluator PT11 rule at COMPOSE time so a verbatim-span breadth surface can
8032	# never ship a decimal-bearing sentence with no adjacent citation (the F "truncated number" garble,
8033	# and the class that would later fail PT11 and abort the report). Reuses external_evaluator's
8034	# abbreviation-aware sentence-boundary helper so "vs." / "e.g." / "Fig." do not split a sentence.
8035	# SUPPRESS-ONLY + DISCLOSED: an uncited-decimal SENTENCE is removed (never a source, never the pool) and
8036	# the removal is disclosed in the section. Faithfulness-safe (removing an UNCITED number is the safe
8037	# direction; nothing is added). Default-ON (PG_COMPOSE_NUMERIC_CITE_GUARANTEE); OFF => byte-identical.
8038	_PT11_DECIMAL_RE = re.compile(r"(?<![A-Za-z0-9.])(-?\d+\.\d+)")
8039	_PT11_CITATION_RE = re.compile(r"\[\d+\]|\[#ev:")
8040	_PT11_EV_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r"\[#ev:[^\]]+\]")
8041	
8042	
8043	def _compose_numeric_cite_guarantee_enabled() -> bool:
8044	    """PT11 kill-switch (``PG_COMPOSE_NUMERIC_CITE_GUARANTEE``). Default-ON; OFF only for an off-value."""
8045	    return (
8046	        os.environ.get("PG_COMPOSE_NUMERIC_CITE_GUARANTEE", "1").strip().lower()
8047	        not in ("0", "false", "off", "no")
8048	    )
8049	
8050	
8051	def _suppress_uncited_decimal_sentences(text: str) -> "tuple[str, list[str]]":
8052	    """Return ``(cleaned_text, removed_sentences)`` — every SENTENCE that carries a decimal number but
8053	    no in-bounds ``[N]`` / ``[#ev:]`` citation is removed (I-deepfix-006 PT11, suppress-only).
8054	
8055	    Splits ``text`` into sentences with external_evaluator's abbreviation-aware boundary helper (the
8056	    SAME unit the PT11 rule scores), so an abbreviation period never splits a sentence. A decimal is
8057	    detected AFTER stripping ``[#ev:…]`` tokens (their integer offsets are not empirical decimals), and
8058	    the citation test reads the RAW sentence (an ``[#ev:]`` provenance token OR an ``[N]`` marker both
8059	    count as cited). Pure, no-network, faithfulness-neutral. Returns the input UNCHANGED (and an empty
8060	    removed list) when nothing qualifies. Fail-safe: if the boundary helper cannot be imported, the text
8061	    is returned untouched (never blind-drop). OFF (``PG_COMPOSE_NUMERIC_CITE_GUARANTEE``) => the input
8062	    is returned unchanged (byte-identical) regardless of content."""
8063	    if not text or not text.strip() or not _compose_numeric_cite_guarantee_enabled():
8064	        return text, []
8065	    try:
8066	        from src.polaris_graph.evaluator.external_evaluator import (  # noqa: PLC0415
8067	            _next_real_sentence_end,
8068	        )
8069	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — cannot split safely -> never suppress blind
8070	        return text, []
8071	    parts: list[str] = []
8072	    pos = 0
8073	    n = len(text)
8074	    while pos < n:
8075	        rel_end = _next_real_sentence_end(text[pos:])
8076	        if rel_end is None:
8077	            parts.append(text[pos:])
8078	            break
8079	        parts.append(text[pos:pos + rel_end])
8080	        pos += rel_end
8081	    kept: list[str] = []
8082	    removed: list[str] = []
8083	    for seg in parts:
8084	        has_citation = bool(_PT11_CITATION_RE.search(seg))
8085	        decimal_scan = _PT11_EV_TOKEN_RE.sub("", seg)
8086	        has_decimal = bool(_PT11_DECIMAL_RE.search(decimal_scan))
8087	        if has_decimal and not has_citation:
8088	            stripped = seg.strip()
8089	            if stripped:
8090	                removed.append(stripped)
8091	            continue
8092	        kept.append(seg)
8093	    if not removed:
8094	        return text, []
8095	    cleaned = re.sub(r"[ \t]{2,}", " ", "".join(kept)).strip()
8096	    return cleaned, removed
8097

8100	# I-deepfix-006 C4 — synthesized body leads; verbatim breadth surfaces -> supporting appendix
8101	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
8102	def _body_lead_enabled() -> bool:
8103	    """C4 kill-switch (``PG_SYNTH_BODY_LEAD``). Default-ON; OFF only for an explicit off-value."""
8104	    return (
8105	        os.environ.get("PG_SYNTH_BODY_LEAD", "1").strip().lower()
8106	        not in ("0", "false", "off", "no")
8107	    )
8108	
8109	
8110	def _reorder_synthesis_body_lead(
8111	    section_results: "list[SectionResult]",
8112	) -> "list[SectionResult]":
8113	    """I-deepfix-006 C4 (``PG_SYNTH_BODY_LEAD``, default ON): stable-partition the section list so the
8114	    synthesized ANALYTICAL sections LEAD the report body and the verbatim-span breadth surfaces —
8115	    ``"Evidence base"`` + ``"Low-relevance evidence (kept at weight)"`` — TRAIL as a clearly-labelled
8116	    supporting appendix.
8117	
8118	    §-1.3 keep-all PLACEMENT, never a drop/cap/thin: every section is still present, and within each
8119	    partition the original relative order is preserved (stable). Faithfulness-neutral — no section
8120	    content, verdict, or count is touched; only the render/assembly order changes. When there is no
8121	    breadth-surface section to move (or the flag is OFF), the SAME list is returned UNCHANGED
8122	    (byte-identical). Fail-safe: if the appendix titles cannot be resolved, the order is left untouched.
8123	    """
8124	    if not section_results or not _body_lead_enabled():
8125	        return section_results
8126	    try:
8127	        from .weighted_enrichment import (  # noqa: PLC0415
8128	            _EVIDENCE_BASE_TITLE,
8129	            _LOW_RELEVANCE_LEDGER_TITLE,
8130	        )
8131	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — cannot classify -> leave order untouched (never reorder blind)
8132	        return section_results
8133	    _supporting_titles = {_EVIDENCE_BASE_TITLE, _LOW_RELEVANCE_LEDGER_TITLE}
8134	    body = [
8135	        s for s in section_results
8136	        if (getattr(s, "title", "") or "") not in _supporting_titles
8137	    ]
8138	    supporting = [
8139	        s for s in section_results
8140	        if (getattr(s, "title", "") or "") in _supporting_titles
8141	    ]
8142	    if not supporting:
8143	        return section_results  # no breadth surface to move -> byte-identical
8144	    return body + supporting
8145	
8146	
8147	def _append_evidence_base_section(
8148	    section_results: "list[SectionResult]",
8149	    global_biblio: "list[dict[str, Any]]",
8150	    ev_ids: "list[str]",
8151	    evidence_pool: "dict[str, Any]",
8152	    research_question: str = "",
8153	    *,
8154	    section_title: str = "",
8155	    section_focus: str = "",
8156	    quantified_models: "dict[tuple[str, str], Any] | None" = None,
8157	) -> bool:
8158	    """I-deepfix-001 WS-3 (#1344) — append the numbered "Evidence base" breadth surface.
8159	
8160	    Renders the FULL ordered unbound-SUPPORTS ``ev_ids`` surface (already UNCAPPED — see
8161	    ``weighted_enrichment.select_unbound_supports_by_weight``) as ONE numbered "Evidence base"
8162	    ``SectionResult`` so every source carrying a surviving isolated-``SUPPORTS`` span gets a real
8163	    ``[N]``. The ``[ev_id]`` markers ``build_evidence_base_section`` emits are resolved to GLOBAL
8164	    ``[N]`` against ``global_biblio``, which is EXTENDED IN PLACE for any newly-surfaced work so the
8165	    downstream Bibliography lists it. §-1.3: this only SURFACES the already-uncapped keep-all set —
8166	    NO cap / floor / thinner is added. Default-ON via ``PG_BREADTH_EVIDENCE_BASE_SECTION`` (checked
8167	    inside ``build_evidence_base_section``); returns ``False`` (no append, byte-identical) when the
8168	    flag is OFF, ``ev_ids`` is empty, or the block is empty.
8169	
8170	    FAITHFULNESS: faithfulness-neutral. Each numbered entry is a VERBATIM isolated-``SUPPORTS`` span
8171	    (``span_verdict == "SUPPORTS"``) — the SAME per-member isolated verification the weighted-
8172	    enrichment section relies on; the frozen faithfulness engine (strict_verify / NLI / 4-role /
8173	    provenance / span-grounding) is UNTOUCHED. Returns ``True`` iff a section was appended.
8174	    """
8175	    from .weighted_enrichment import (  # noqa: PLC0415
8176	        _EVIDENCE_BASE_TITLE,
8177	        build_evidence_base_section,
8178	    )
8179	    from .live_deepseek_generator import _rewrite_draft_with_spans  # noqa: PLC0415
8180	    from .provenance_generator import (  # noqa: PLC0415
8181	        resolve_provenance_to_citations_with_count,
8182	        strict_verify,
8183	    )
8184	
8185	    # I-deepfix-001 (#1344 SPAN-TOPICALITY): thread the research question so a confidently-
8186	    # foreign SPAN inside an on-topic source is WITHHELD from the numbered breadth surface
8187	    # (§-1.3 WITHHOLD-and-disclose per span; the source stays in evidence_pool + disclosure).
8188	    block = build_evidence_base_section(
8189	        ev_ids, evidence_pool, research_question=research_question
8190	    )  # flag-gated internally
8191	    if not block or not block.strip():
8192	        return False
8193	
8194	    # I-deepfix-001 WS-3 P1 FIX (Codex iter-1: "Evidence base bypasses the frozen verification path").
8195	    # A breadth-surface line MUST NOT ship without passing the frozen faithfulness gate. Route the
8196	    # verbatim-span block through the SAME `_rewrite_draft_with_spans` + `strict_verify` the sections
8197	    # use (the FIX-K verbatim-span pattern @4214). Strip build_evidence_base_section's OWN
8198	    # "## Evidence base" header + leading "N. " display numbers so strict_verify sees clean span
8199	    # sentences; the [ev_id] markers resolve to real [#ev:...] provenance tokens exactly like the FIX-K
8200	    # draft. Each surviving sentence is a strict_verify-VERIFIED span carrying a real provenance token
8201	    # -> a genuine verified claim the downstream 4-role D8 gate judges (native_gate_b_inputs reads
8202	    # SectionResult.kept_sentences_pre_resolve), NOT a line shipping outside strict_verify/D8.
8203	    # §-1.3: SURFACE the already-uncapped keep-all SUPPORTS set; a line that CANNOT ground is DROPPED
8204	    # by the frozen gate (never padded, never fabricated). The frozen engine is UNTOUCHED (called, not
8205	    # edited). Default-ON via the flag checked inside build_evidence_base_section.
8206	    _own_header = f"## {_EVIDENCE_BASE_TITLE}\n\n"
8207	    draft = block[len(_own_header):] if block.startswith(_own_header) else block
8208	    draft = re.sub(r"(?m)^\s*\d+\.\s+", "", draft)  # drop the display "N. " prefixes -> clean span lines
8209	    if not draft.strip():
8210	        return False
8211	
8212	    rewritten, _converted, _unverified = _rewrite_draft_with_spans(draft, evidence_pool)
8213	    # MOAT SEAM (2026-07-11): pass the verified quantified-model registry so a computed
8214	    # ``[#calc:]`` number surfaced in the evidence-base breadth block routes to the Regime-C
8215	    # calc verifier instead of dropping ``no_provenance_token``. Default None => byte-identical
8216	    # legacy path. Faithfulness-neutral: the ``[#ev:]`` span gate is untouched.
8217	    report = strict_verify(rewritten, evidence_pool, quantified_models=quantified_models)
8218	    kept_verified = [v for v in (report.kept_sentences or []) if getattr(v, "is_verified", False)]
8219	    if not kept_verified:
8220	        return False  # nothing survived the frozen gate -> NO section (no unverified breadth ships)
8221	
8222	    local_text, local_biblio, emitted = resolve_provenance_to_citations_with_count(
8223	        report.kept_sentences, evidence_pool,
8224	    )
8225	    if emitted <= 0 or not local_text.strip():
8226	        return False
8227	
8228	    # The section is appended AFTER the global bibliography remap, so map the resolver's LOCAL [N]
8229	    # onto the GLOBAL numbering here; extend global_biblio for any newly-surfaced work (§-1.3 keep-all,
8230	    # never drops a source).
8231	    ev_to_gnum: dict[str, int] = {
8232	        str(b.get("evidence_id", "")): b.get("num")
8233	        for b in (global_biblio or [])
8234	        if b.get("evidence_id")
8235	    }
8236	    next_gnum = [max((int(b.get("num", 0) or 0) for b in (global_biblio or [])), default=0) + 1]
8237	    local_to_global: dict[int, int] = {}
8238	    for row in (local_biblio or []):
8239	        eid = str(row.get("evidence_id", ""))
8240	        lnum = row.get("num")
8241	        if lnum is None or not eid:
8242	            continue
8243	        gnum = ev_to_gnum.get(eid)
8244	        if gnum is None:
8245	            gnum = next_gnum[0]
8246	            next_gnum[0] += 1
8247	            ev_to_gnum[eid] = gnum
8248	            global_biblio.append({
8249	                "num": gnum,
8250	                "evidence_id": eid,
8251	                "url": row.get("url", ""),
8252	                "tier": row.get("tier", ""),
8253	                "statement": row.get("statement", ""),
8254	            })
8255	        local_to_global[int(lnum)] = gnum
8256	
8257	    def _local_to_global_marker(match: "re.Match") -> str:
8258	        lnum = int(match.group(1))
8259	        return f"[{local_to_global.get(lnum, lnum)}]"
8260	
8261	    body = re.sub(r"\[(\d+)\]", _local_to_global_marker, local_text).strip()
8262	    if not body:
8263	        return False
8264	
8265	    # I-deepfix-006 PT11 (PG_COMPOSE_NUMERIC_CITE_GUARANTEE, default ON): compose-time numeric-citation
8266	    # guarantee for the verbatim-span breadth surface (Evidence base + low-relevance ledger — both routed
8267	    # through here at final assembly). Every span sentence normally carries an [#ev:] provenance token
8268	    # that resolved to a global [N] above, so this is a SAFETY BACKSTOP: a decimal-bearing sentence that
8269	    # lost its citation (the F "truncated number" garble) is SUPPRESSED (never a source drop) and the
8270	    # removal is DISCLOSED in the section — never ships an uncited number that would later fail PT11 and
8271	    # abort the report. Faithfulness-neutral (suppress-only, no add). OFF => byte-identical.
8272	    if _compose_numeric_cite_guarantee_enabled():
8273	        body, _pt11_removed = _suppress_uncited_decimal_sentences(body)
8274	        if _pt11_removed:
8275	            body = body.strip()
8276	            logger.warning(
8277	                "[multi_section] I-deepfix-006 PT11 numeric-citation guarantee: suppressed %d "
8278	                "uncited-decimal sentence(s) from %r (compose-time backstop, disclosed): %s",
8279	                len(_pt11_removed), (section_title or _EVIDENCE_BASE_TITLE),
8280	                " | ".join(s[:80] for s in _pt11_removed[:5]),
8281	            )
8282	            if not body:
8283	                return False  # every span line was an uncited decimal -> no section (nothing verified ships)
8284	            body = (
8285	                f"{body}\n\n_Numeric-citation guarantee: {len(_pt11_removed)} sentence(s) carrying an "
8286	                "uncited decimal number were suppressed from this breadth surface at compose time "
8287	                "(PG_COMPOSE_NUMERIC_CITE_GUARANTEE)._"
8288	            )
8289	
8290	    # I-deepfix-001 U17 (#1335): NEAR-DUPLICATE collapse. The Corroborated Weighted Findings
8291	    # section (built from the SAME uncapped unbound-SUPPORTS ev_ids surface) renders the same
8292	    # verified spans as this Evidence base body — measured 83-94% identical. If an already-assembled
8293	    # section is near-identical, DON'T append this second rendering (keep ONE). Faithfulness-neutral:
8294	    # every span here already passed the frozen strict_verify + 4-role D8 gate above, and the
8295	    # surviving section carries the SAME spans + SAME [N] citations (same sources => breadth kept).
8296	    # Kill-switch PG_SECTION_DEDUP_ENABLED (default ON) => OFF is byte-identical legacy (both render).
8297	    _dup_title = _section_body_is_near_duplicate(body, section_results)
8298	    if _dup_title is not None:
8299	        logger.info(
8300	            "[multi_section] I-deepfix-001 U17 near-duplicate collapse: Evidence base body is "
8301	            ">= %.2f similar to already-rendered section %r (same uncapped SUPPORTS surface) — "
8302	            "SKIPPING the redundant second rendering (breadth preserved: same spans + same [N] in "
8303	            "the kept section; frozen faithfulness engine untouched)",
8304	            _section_dedup_similarity_threshold(), _dup_title,
8305	        )
8306	        return False
8307	
8308	    # I-deepfix-001 F2 (#1371): the SectionResult TITLE/FOCUS are parametrized so the SAME frozen
8309	    # verified-span render path can also emit the "Low-relevance evidence (kept at weight)" ledger
8310	    # (furniture / off-topic / below-floor entries MOVED below the appendix boundary — §-1.3 placement,
8311	    # never a drop). The block's OWN "## Evidence base" header strip above stays bound to
8312	    # ``_EVIDENCE_BASE_TITLE`` (that is what ``build_evidence_base_section`` emits); only the rendered
8313	    # SECTION title/focus differ. Empty params => byte-identical legacy Evidence base section.
8314	    _section_title = section_title or _EVIDENCE_BASE_TITLE
8315	    _section_focus = section_focus or (
8316	        "Breadth surface: every source carrying a surviving isolated-SUPPORTS span, each entry "
8317	        "strict_verify-VERIFIED and 4-role D8-judged (routed through the frozen gate, no bypass)."
8318	    )
8319	    section_results.append(SectionResult(
8320	        title=_section_title,
8321	        focus=_section_focus,
8322	        ev_ids_assigned=[str(e) for e in (ev_ids or []) if e],
8323	        raw_draft=block,
8324	        rewritten_draft=rewritten,
8325	        verified_text=body,
8326	        biblio_slice=[],
8327	        sentences_verified=emitted,
8328	        sentences_dropped=(
8329	            report.total_dropped
8330	            + max(0, (report.total_kept or len(report.kept_sentences)) - emitted)
8331	        ),
8332	        regen_attempted=False,
8333	        dropped_due_to_failure=False,
8334	        # THE P1 FIX: real strict_verify SentenceVerification objects so native_gate_b_inputs promotes
8335	        # each VERIFIED entry to a FourRoleClaim and the 4-role D8 gate judges it (no bypass).
8336	        kept_sentences_pre_resolve=list(report.kept_sentences),
8337	    ))
8338	    logger.info(
8339	        "[multi_section] WS-3 Evidence base: %d strict_verify-VERIFIED source entr(ies) routed "
8340	        "through strict_verify + 4-role D8 (uncapped SUPPORTS surface -> global [N])",
8341	        emitted,
8342	    )
8343	    return True
8344	
8345	
8346	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
8347	# M-44 (2026-04-22): scorer/subset primary-trial boost + same-sentence
8348	# validator. Codex V28 plan pass-2 APPROVED.
8349	#
8350	# Gap addressed: V27 cited SURPASS-2 via T4 post-hoc and omitted
8351	# SURPASS-CVOT + SURMOUNT-1..4 entirely despite primaries being in
8352	# the evidence subset. Root cause at the generator stage is that the
8353	# outline planner picked post-hocs/meta-analyses over primaries on
8354	# generic relevance scoring.
8355	#
8356	# Pre-M-44 M-20 had a prompt-only trial-specific citation rule that
8357	# failed in practice. M-44 adds section-subset INJECTION (forcing
8358	# primary ev_ids into sections discussing a named trial) + post-
8359	# generation SAME-SENTENCE VALIDATOR (named trial + matching primary
8360	# ev_id must be cited in same or adjacent sentence) + one regen on
8361	# validator fail.
8362	#
8363	# Scope: applies only to Efficacy, Comparative, Safety, Weight Loss,
8364	# Long-term Outcomes sections. Regulatory / Contradictions /
8365	# Limitations / Methods / Mechanism are excluded (primaries not
8366	# authoritative for those).
8367	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
8368	
8369	_M44_PRIMARY_ELIGIBLE_SECTIONS: set[str] = {
8370	    "efficacy",
8371	    "safety",
8372	    "comparative",
8373	    "dose response",
8374	    "population subgroups",
8375	    "long-term outcomes",
8376	}

8378	# Section-title tokens that indicate a Weight-loss framing. Matches
8379	# Codex plan §M-44 section scope. Not in _ALLOWED_SECTIONS directly
8380	# (Weight-loss framing typically lands under Efficacy or Population
8381	# Subgroups), but we keep the keyword in case future outlines add it.
8382	_M44_WEIGHT_TOKENS = frozenset({"weight", "obesity", "adipos", "bmi"})
8383

8385	def _m44_section_is_primary_eligible(section_title: str) -> bool:
8386	    """M-44 (2026-04-22): True iff this section title qualifies for
8387	    primary-trial citation floor. Case-insensitive lower-match."""
8388	    t = (section_title or "").lower().strip()
8389	    if t in _M44_PRIMARY_ELIGIBLE_SECTIONS:
8390	        return True
8391	    # Tolerate weight-loss framing under any section title.
8392	    return any(tok in t for tok in _M44_WEIGHT_TOKENS)

8395	# I-meta-005 Phase 1 (#985, P2 note B): on-mode archetype-keyed routing for the
8396	# post-generation primary-trial validator. The archetypes that carry
8397	# quantitative empirical claims (where named-study same-sentence citation
8398	# matters) are field-invariant tags — NOT clinical title literals — so the
8399	# zero-clinical-literal guard (P1-10) whitelists them.

8431	def _m53_compute_primary_custody_log(
8432	    primary_trial_anchors: list[str] | None,
8433	    live_corpus: list[dict[str, Any]] | None,
8434	    evidence_pool: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
8435	    section_results: list["SectionResult"],
8436	    global_biblio: list[dict[str, Any]],
8437	    m44_injection_log: list[dict[str, Any]],
8438	) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
8439	    """M-53 (2026-04-23): V29-c per-anchor custody telemetry.
8440	
8441	    Codex plan pass-1 revisions #6-7 woven in:
8442	    - Retain all 9 fields.
8443	    - Compute `selected_into_pool` by canonical ev_id/key membership
8444	      in the final `evidence_pool` (single source of truth).
8445	    - Compute `cited_in_verified_prose` AFTER bibliography numbering
8446	      is finalized, using the ev_id → biblio number mapping that
8447	      rendered the report.
8448	
8449	    Returns list of 9-field dicts, one per configured anchor. Empty
8450	    list when no anchors configured.
8451	    """
8452	    if not primary_trial_anchors:
8453	        return []
8454	    from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.evidence_selector import (
8455	        _m42e_detect_primary_for_anchor,
8456	    )
8457	
8458	    # Build ev_id → biblio_num mapping (finalized bibliography)
8459	    ev_id_to_biblio_num: dict[str, int] = {}
8460	    for entry in global_biblio:
8461	        evid = entry.get("evidence_id")
8462	        num = entry.get("num")
8463	        if isinstance(evid, str) and isinstance(num, int):
8464	            ev_id_to_biblio_num[evid] = num
8465	
8466	    # Build injection log lookup: anchor → list of section titles
8467	    injections_by_anchor: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
8468	    for entry in m44_injection_log:
8469	        anchor = entry.get("anchor")
8470	        section = entry.get("section")
8471	        action = entry.get("action", "")
8472	        if not isinstance(anchor, str) or not anchor:
8473	            continue
8474	        # Count any action that places an ev_id into a section
8475	        # (injected, swap_in_for_*, already_present, injected_from_corpus
8476	        # at the pool level also counts as "injected_into_pool")
8477	        if action in ("injected", "already_present") or action.startswith(
8478	            "swap_in_for_"
8479	        ) or action == "injected_from_corpus":
8480	            injections_by_anchor.setdefault(anchor, [])
8481	            if isinstance(section, str) and section and section not in (
8482	                "<pool-level>", *injections_by_anchor[anchor]
8483	            ):
8484	                injections_by_anchor[anchor].append(section)
8485	
8486	    # Deduplicate anchors preserving order
8487	    unique_anchors: list[str] = []
8488	    seen: set[str] = set()
8489	    for a in primary_trial_anchors:
8490	        if a not in seen:
8491	            seen.add(a)
8492	            unique_anchors.append(a)
8493	
8494	    out: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
8495	    for anchor in unique_anchors:
8496	        # Found in live_corpus?
8497	        found_row = None
8498	        for row in (live_corpus or []):
8499	            if _m42e_detect_primary_for_anchor(row, anchor):
8500	                found_row = row
8501	                break
8502	        found_in_corpus = found_row is not None
8503	        found_ev_id = (
8504	            found_row.get("evidence_id")
8505	            if found_row and isinstance(found_row.get("evidence_id"), str)
8506	            else ""
8507	        )
8508	
8509	        # Selected into pool? (Scan pool for anchor-matched rows,
8510	        # using canonical ev_id/content-key identity — not dict membership.)
8511	        selected_ev_id: str = ""
8512	        for ev_id, pool_row in evidence_pool.items():
8513	            if _m42e_detect_primary_for_anchor(pool_row, anchor):
8514	                selected_ev_id = ev_id
8515	                break
8516	        selected_into_pool = bool(selected_ev_id)
8517	
8518	        # Injected into which section(s)? Use injection log.
8519	        injected_sections = injections_by_anchor.get(anchor, [])
8520	        injected_into_section = (
8521	            injected_sections[0] if injected_sections else None
8522	        )
8523	
8524	        # Direct quote adequacy (from pool row if selected, else from
8525	        # found_row if only in corpus).
8526	        ref_row = (
8527	            evidence_pool.get(selected_ev_id)
8528	            if selected_ev_id
8529	            else found_row
8530	        )
8531	        direct_quote_chars = (
8532	            len(ref_row.get("direct_quote", ""))
8533	            if ref_row else 0
8534	        )
8535	        direct_quote_adequate = direct_quote_chars >= 100
8536	
8537	        # Cited in verified prose? Check bibliography-num citations in
8538	        # each section's verified_text. Uses ev_id → biblio_num map
8539	        # that rendered the report.
8540	        citation_count = 0
8541	        if selected_ev_id and selected_ev_id in ev_id_to_biblio_num:
8542	            biblio_num = ev_id_to_biblio_num[selected_ev_id]
8543	            import re as _re
8544	            pattern = _re.compile(rf"\[{biblio_num}\]")
8545	            for sr in section_results:
8546	                if sr.dropped_due_to_failure or not sr.verified_text:
8547	                    continue
8548	                citation_count += len(pattern.findall(sr.verified_text))
8549	        cited_in_verified_prose = citation_count > 0
8550	
8551	        out.append({
8552	            "anchor": anchor,
8553	            "found_in_live_corpus": found_in_corpus,
8554	            "found_ev_id": found_ev_id,
8555	            "selected_into_pool": selected_into_pool,
8556	            "injected_into_section": injected_into_section,
8557	            "direct_quote_chars": direct_quote_chars,
8558	            "direct_quote_adequate": direct_quote_adequate,
8559	            "cited_in_verified_prose": cited_in_verified_prose,
8560	            "citation_count": citation_count,
8561	        })
8562	    return out

8565	def _m52_pull_from_live_corpus(
8566	    evidence_pool: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
8567	    live_corpus: list[dict[str, Any]] | None,
8568	    primary_trial_anchors: list[str],
8569	) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
8570	    """M-52 (2026-04-23): V29 Strategy β cycle 1, item 2. Belt-and-
8571	    suspenders companion to M-51. Pulls anchor-matched primary rows
8572	    from `live_corpus` into `evidence_pool` when the selector-
8573	    enforced M-51 hard-reservation failed (e.g. selector called
8574	    without `primary_trial_anchors` param, or selector bug).
8575	
8576	    Codex plan pass-1 revisions #4-5 woven in:
8577	    - Preserve existing live-corpus `evidence_id` when present and
8578	      not colliding with a different row already in evidence_pool.
8579	    - Fallback `ev_from_corpus_{anchor_slug}_{n}` ONLY for rows
8580	      missing evidence_id OR colliding with a different row.
8581	    - Pulled rows must carry all fields strict_verify + bibliography
8582	      rendering need: evidence_id, direct_quote, source_url, title,
8583	      tier. Rows missing any required field are skipped (fail-loud,
8584	      not silent mutation).
8585	
8586	    Mutates `evidence_pool` in place; returns list of pulled row
8587	    dicts (newly added entries) for telemetry.
8588	    """
8589	    if not live_corpus or not primary_trial_anchors:
8590	        return []
8591	    from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.evidence_selector import (
8592	        _m42e_detect_primary_for_anchor,
8593	    )
8594	    pulled: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
8595	    # Track existing ev_ids + canonical keys in the pool
8596	    pool_ev_ids = set(evidence_pool.keys())
8597	
8598	    def _content_canon(row: dict[str, Any]) -> tuple:
8599	        """Content-identity (ignores evidence_id): for collision
8600	        detection when two rows share an ID but differ in content."""
8601	        url = (row.get("source_url") or row.get("url") or "").lower()
8602	        title_text = ""
8603	        for k in ("title", "statement", "source_title"):
8604	            v = row.get(k)
8605	            if isinstance(v, str) and v:
8606	                title_text = v
8607	                break
8608	        dq = (row.get("direct_quote") or "")[:200]
8609	        return ("key", url, title_text.lower()[:200], dq)
8610	
8611	    pool_content_canon = {
8612	        _content_canon(row): ev_id
8613	        for ev_id, row in evidence_pool.items()
8614	    }
8615	
8616	    def _anchor_slug(anchor: str) -> str:
8617	        # For ev_from_corpus_<slug>_<n>: lowercase + replace non-alphanum
8618	        return "".join(c if c.isalnum() else "_" for c in anchor.lower())
8619	
8620	    for anchor in primary_trial_anchors:
8621	        # Already have a primary for this anchor in the pool?
8622	        have_it = any(
8623	            _m42e_detect_primary_for_anchor(row, anchor)
8624	            for row in evidence_pool.values()
8625	        )
8626	        if have_it:
8627	            continue
8628	        # Find best candidate in live_corpus
8629	        for corpus_row in live_corpus:
8630	            if not _m42e_detect_primary_for_anchor(corpus_row, anchor):
8631	                continue
8632	            # Codex revision #5: require strict_verify-essential fields
8633	            required = ("direct_quote", "tier")
8634	            if any(not corpus_row.get(f) for f in required):
8635	                continue
8636	            # Prefer url field; build effective source_url if missing
8637	            src_url = corpus_row.get("source_url") or corpus_row.get("url")
8638	            if not src_url:
8639	                continue
8640	            # Content canonical key (ignores evidence_id) —
8641	            # detects "same row already in pool, different id".
8642	            content_key = _content_canon(corpus_row)
8643	            if content_key in pool_content_canon:
8644	                # Same content already in pool under some id; skip.
8645	                continue
8646	            # Codex revision #4: preserve live-corpus evidence_id when
8647	            # present AND not colliding with a DIFFERENT row in pool.
8648	            corpus_evid = corpus_row.get("evidence_id")
8649	            if (
8650	                isinstance(corpus_evid, str)
8651	                and corpus_evid
8652	                and corpus_evid not in pool_ev_ids
8653	            ):
8654	                ev_id = corpus_evid
8655	            else:
8656	                # Collision (existing pool row uses this ID for
8657	                # different content) OR missing ID → prefixed fallback
8658	                base = f"ev_from_corpus_{_anchor_slug(anchor)}"
8659	                n = 0
8660	                ev_id = base
8661	                while ev_id in pool_ev_ids:
8662	                    n += 1
8663	                    ev_id = f"{base}_{n}"
8664	            # Build the row to add — ensure all required fields plus
8665	            # preserved title/source_url. Use a shallow copy to avoid
8666	            # mutating the live_corpus entry.
8667	            new_row = dict(corpus_row)
8668	            new_row["evidence_id"] = ev_id
8669	            new_row["source_url"] = src_url
8670	            # Title accessor fallback (M-48 live-row schema): prefer
8671	            # title, else statement.
8672	            if not new_row.get("title"):
8673	                for k in ("statement", "source_title"):
8674	                    v = new_row.get(k)
8675	                    if isinstance(v, str) and v:
8676	                        new_row["title"] = v
8677	                        break
8678	            evidence_pool[ev_id] = new_row
8679	            pool_ev_ids.add(ev_id)
8680	            pool_content_canon[content_key] = ev_id
8681	            pulled.append({
8682	                "anchor": anchor,
8683	                "evidence_id": ev_id,
8684	                "source_url": src_url,
8685	                "preserved_live_corpus_id": (
8686	                    isinstance(corpus_evid, str) and corpus_evid
8687	                    and corpus_evid == ev_id
8688	                ),
8689	            })
8690	            break  # one primary per anchor
8691	    return pulled

8694	def _m44_detect_primary_ev_ids(
8695	    evidence_pool: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
8696	    primary_trial_anchors: list[str],
8697	) -> dict[str, list[str]]:
8698	    """M-44 (2026-04-22): for each anchor, list the ev_ids in the pool
8699	    that match as a primary-trial row.
8700	
8701	    Uses the same `_m42e_detect_primary_for_anchor` predicate the
8702	    selector uses, so detection is consistent across selector and
8703	    generator.
8704	
8705	    Returns dict keyed by anchor → list of ev_id strings. Only anchors
8706	    with ≥1 matching row are included.
8707	
8708	    M-52 (V29-b) extension: caller should run
8709	    `_m52_pull_from_live_corpus(evidence_pool, live_corpus, anchors)`
8710	    BEFORE this function so any missing primaries in the pool have
8711	    been pulled from live_corpus. This function only scans
8712	    `evidence_pool` (single source of truth after M-52 pull).
8713	    """
8714	    from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.evidence_selector import (
8715	        _m42e_detect_primary_for_anchor,
8716	    )
8717	    out: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
8718	    if not primary_trial_anchors:
8719	        return out
8720	    for anchor in primary_trial_anchors:
8721	        matches = []
8722	        for ev_id, row in evidence_pool.items():
8723	            if _m42e_detect_primary_for_anchor(row, anchor):
8724	                matches.append(ev_id)
8725	        if matches:
8726	            out[anchor] = matches
8727	    return out

8730	# M-44 pass-2 (Codex audit medium #3): per-anchor → section-focus
8731	# affinity. Rather than flattening all primaries into every eligible
8732	# section, match anchor against section title/focus tokens so CVOT
8733	# lands in Safety / Cardiovascular sections, SURMOUNT lands in
8734	# Weight-loss / Population-Subgroups, SURPASS lands in Efficacy /
8735	# Comparative. Generic SURPASS (glycemic primary) still falls through
8736	# to Efficacy by default when no specific match found.
8737	_M44_ANCHOR_SECTION_AFFINITY: dict[str, frozenset[str]] = {
8738	    # Cardiovascular outcomes trials → Safety + Long-term Outcomes
8739	    # (captured via "cardiovascular", "cvot", "mace" tokens)
8740	    "_cardiovascular": frozenset({"safety", "long-term outcomes"}),
8741	    # Weight-loss trials (SURMOUNT) → Weight/Population-Subgroups/Efficacy
8742	    "_weight": frozenset({
8743	        "efficacy", "population subgroups", "long-term outcomes",
8744	    }),
8745	    # Default (general efficacy like SURPASS) → broad eligible set
8746	    "_general": frozenset({
8747	        "efficacy", "comparative", "safety", "dose response",
8748	        "population subgroups", "long-term outcomes",
8749	    }),
8750	}
